{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Base directory for this skill: /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude/skills/session-workflow\n\n# Session Workflow\n\nThis skill expands each step of the core loop: **READ → REVIEW → DECIDE → ACT → VERIFY → DOCUMENT**.\n\n---\n\n## 1. READ — Load Context\n\n- Check if `REPOSITORY.md` exists. If not, invoke the `repo-bootstrap` skill before proceeding.\n- Read `.claude-beat/PRD.md`, `.claude-beat/memory/MEMORY.md`, `.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md`\n- If PRD.md references additional files, read those too\n- Read the last 2–3 session logs from `.claude-beat/logs/sessions/`\n- Fetch open issues and PRs via `gh` CLI\n\n---\n\n## 2. REVIEW — Assess Current State\n\nBefore picking new work, check what needs attention right now.\n\n### 2a. Production Health Check (every session)\n\nIf the project has a production or staging environment:\n\n1. Identify the production URL (from memory, `.env`, deploy config, or README). If no production URL can be found, skip the production health check and note \"No production environment detected\" in the session log.\n2. Use `which agent-browser` to locate the browser tool. Always attempt browser verification before falling back to non-visual checks. Open the production URL and verify core functionality works.\n3. Check for visible errors, broken pages, or degraded behavior\n4. If the platform provides logs or monitoring (Render, Vercel, etc.) — check recent error logs\n\n**If production issues are found:**\n- **Critical** (app down, core flow broken, data loss risk): fix immediately in this session, create a PR, deploy\n- **Medium** (non-blocking bugs, UI glitches, non-critical errors): create a GitHub issue labeled `approved`, `bug` with priority `P1`\n- **Low** (cosmetic, minor warnings): create a GitHub issue labeled `approved`, `bug` with priority `P2`\n\n### 2b. Follow Up on Previous Sessions\n\nRead the most recent session logs and check for:\n\n- **Deployments**: if something was deployed last session, verify it is live and working (part of 2a)\n- **Human action requests**: if a previous session created a `needs-human` or `blocked` issue, check whether the human has acted on it\n  - If resolved — acknowledge, close the issue, continue with any unblocked work\n  - If stale (no response for 2+ sessions) — add a comment on the issue reminding the owner, and note it in the session log\n- **Open PRs with review comments**: if a PR has unaddressed reviewer feedback, respond and update before opening new PRs\n- **WIP branches**: if the previous session left work-in-progress, pick it up and finish it\n\n### 2c. PRD Sync\n\nScan PRD (and any referenced spec files) for features with no corresponding GitHub issue. For each missing feature:\n\n- Label: `approved` + `prd` + priority (`P0`/`P1`/`P2` matching the PRD)\n- Title prefix: `prd:` (e.g., `prd: add CEFR level selector`)\n- These are pre-approved — no proposal step, no waiting for human reaction\n\nSearch existing issues first to avoid duplicates (`gh issue list --state all`). This step runs every session regardless of other work.\n\n---\n\n## 3. DECIDE — Pick What to Work On\n\nWrite a brief triage summary in your session log before starting work:\n- Production status (healthy / issues found)\n- Follow-ups resolved or escalated\n- What you will work on this session and why\n\nSelect work in this priority order:\n\n1. **Critical production issues** found during REVIEW\n2. **Approved `P0` issues** — launch blockers, critical bugs, critical PRD features\n3. **Open PRs needing attention** — review comments, merge-ready PRs, CI failures\n4. **In-progress work** from previous sessions (WIP branches, draft PRs)\n5. **Approved `P1` and `P2` issues** — P1 first, then P2\n6. **Stale proposals** — if a `proposal` issue has had no human reaction for 2+ sessions, change label to `approved` and comment \"Auto-approving after no objection.\" Never self-approve issues labeled `needs-human` or `blocked`.\n\n---\n\n## 4. ACT — Execute\n\nFor each piece of work:\n1. Implement the change\n2. Run lint, tests, build\n3. Test using `agent-browser` skill if UI-related\n4. Commit and create/update PR (use `pr-lifecycle` skill)\n5. If the primary item finishes quickly and there is a small, self-contained second item, you may take it on. Do not start a third item — leave it for the next session.\n\n---\n\n## 5. VERIFY — Post-Deploy Check\n\nIf you deployed anything this session:\n1. Verify the deployment succeeded\n2. If browser testing tooling is available, open production and confirm the change is live\n3. Check for regressions — test core flows, not just the new feature\n\nIf you opened or updated a PR this session:\n- Verify CI checks are passing before closing the session\n\nIf you did NOT deploy but production had issues in REVIEW:\n- Confirm your fix PR is open and ready for review\n\n---\n\n## 6. DOCUMENT — Close the Session\n\n1. Write session log to `.claude-beat/logs/sessions/YYYY-MM-DD_HH-MM.md`\n2. Update `.claude-beat/memory/MEMORY.md`\n3. Update `.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md` if you learned something new\n4. Ensure no uncommitted changes remain (`git status`)\n5. All branches pushed, all work has a PR (draft is fine for WIP)\n\n---\n\n## Quick Reference: Session Checklist\n\n```\nREAD\n[ ] Context loaded (PRD, memory, logs, issues, PRs)\n\nREVIEW\n[ ] Production health checked\n[ ] Previous session follow-ups addressed\n\nDECIDE\n[ ] Triage summary written\n[ ] Work selected by priority\n\nACT\n[ ] Changes implemented, tested, PR opened\n\nVERIFY\n[ ] CI checks passing (if PR opened)\n[ ] Production check (if deployed)\n\nDOCUMENT\n[ ] Session log written\n[ ] Memory updated\n[ ] No uncommitted changes\n```\n"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"235c9f17-c672-4220-8162-6bda60e23e6c","uuid":"eac833db-d209-4182-ad66-5d5b99519dc1","timestamp":"2026-03-19T23:00:07.982Z","isSynthetic":true}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_019hifeb7RLFHSUW86W391ib","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Product Requirements Document\n     2→\n     3→## Project Name\n     4→\n     5→Bookmate\n     6→\n     7→## Overview\n     8→\n     9→Bookmate is a web service that helps fiction readers find companions for reading and discussing books together. Users post listings for books they want to read, others join, and once a group forms, they connect via Telegram to coordinate their reading sessions. Think of it as matchmaking for book clubs.\n    10→\n    11→**Target audience:** English-speaking fiction readers who want structured reading companionship but struggle to find like-minded partners.\n    12→\n    13→## Goals\n    14→\n    15→- Make it effortless to find people who want to read the same book at the same pace\n    16→- Remove the friction of organizing a reading group — from discovery to first meeting\n    17→- Keep the platform lightweight: match readers, hand off to Telegram for discussion\n    18→\n    19→## P0 — Must Have (Core, launch blocker)\n    20→\n    21→### User Authentication\n    22→\n    23→Email + password registration and login. Required to post or join any listing.\n    24→\n    25→### Create a Reading Listing\n    26→\n    27→A user creates a listing with:\n    28→- **Book** — selected via Open Library API (title, author, cover art auto-populated)\n    29→- **Preferred language** — language for discussion\n    30→- **Reading pace** — e.g., \"1 chapter per week\", \"50 pages per day\"\n    31→- **Start date** — when the group begins reading\n    32→- **Meeting format** — voice calls, text chat, or mixed\n    33→- **Max group size** — author sets the cap (e.g., 3–6 people)\n    34→\n    35→### Browse Listings\n    36→\n    37→Simple chronological list of open listings. No search or filters at this stage — just a scrollable feed of available reading groups to join.\n    38→\n    39→### Join a Listing\n    40→\n    41→Any authenticated user can join an open listing. First-come-first-served, no approval step. A user can be part of multiple reading groups simultaneously.\n    42→\n    43→### Group Formation\n    44→\n    45→When a listing reaches its max group size:\n    46→- The listing becomes invisible to non-participants (effectively closed)\n    47→- The author is prompted to share a Telegram group link with participants\n    48→- The author manually creates the Telegram group and pastes the invite link\n    49→\n    50→### Notifications\n    51→\n    52→Email + in-app notifications for:\n    53→- **Each new sign-up** — listing author is notified when someone joins\n    54→- **Group full** — all participants are notified when the group reaches its cap\n    55→\n    56→### User Profile (Basic)\n    57→\n    58→- Display name\n    59→- Short bio\n    60→\n    61→## P1 — Should Have (Important, not launch blocker)\n    62→\n    63→### Search and Filters\n    64→\n    65→- Search listings by book title or author\n    66→- Filter by reading pace, start date, meeting format\n    67→- Browsable feed with sorting options\n    68→\n    69→### Telegram Group Creation Assistance\n    70→\n    71→Provide guidance or tooling to help the listing author create and share the Telegram group link more easily (e.g., step-by-step instructions, link validation).\n    72→\n    73→### Extended User Profile\n    74→\n    75→- Reading history (past groups participated in)\n    76→- \"Currently reading\" list\n    77→- Favorite genres\n    78→\n    79→## P2 — Nice to Have (Bonus, if time permits)\n    80→\n    81→### Automatic Telegram Group Creation\n    82→\n    83→System auto-creates a Telegram group via API and shares the invite link with all participants — no manual step needed.\n    84→\n    85→### Applicant Approval System\n    86→\n    87→Listing author can review and approve/reject users who want to join, instead of first-come-first-served.\n    88→\n    89→### Rating and Reputation System\n    90→\n    91→Users can rate their reading group experience. Build a reputation score based on participation history and peer ratings.\n    92→\n    93→### Discord Support\n    94→\n    95→Allow group creation via Discord as an alternative to Telegram.\n    96→\n    97→## Non-Goals\n    98→\n    99→- No payment or monetization features\n   100→- No mobile app (web only)\n   101→- No real-time chat within the platform (groups communicate via Telegram)\n   102→- No complex third-party integrations beyond Open Library API\n   103→- No AI-powered recommendations or matching (AI is allowed where it genuinely improves UX)\n   104→- No content hosting (e-books, PDFs, etc.)\n   105→\n   106→## Tech Stack\n   107→\n   108→- **Frontend + Backend:** Next.js (full-stack)\n   109→- **Database:** SQLite (local, persistent across container restarts)\n   110→- **Analytics:** SQLite-based (no third-party analytics)\n   111→- **Deployment:** Docker container with mounted volume for SQLite persistence\n   112→- **Book data:** Open Library API (free, no API key required)\n   113→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"235c9f17-c672-4220-8162-6bda60e23e6c","uuid":"d6fd07b2-7fc1-46c2-8495-6c24ec93bf84","timestamp":"2026-03-19T23:00:13.697Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/PRD.md","content":"# Product Requirements Document\n\n## Project Name\n\nBookmate\n\n## Overview\n\nBookmate is a web service that helps fiction readers find companions for reading and discussing books together. Users post listings for books they want to read, others join, and once a group forms, they connect via Telegram to coordinate their reading sessions. Think of it as matchmaking for book clubs.\n\n**Target audience:** English-speaking fiction readers who want structured reading companionship but struggle to find like-minded partners.\n\n## Goals\n\n- Make it effortless to find people who want to read the same book at the same pace\n- Remove the friction of organizing a reading group — from discovery to first meeting\n- Keep the platform lightweight: match readers, hand off to Telegram for discussion\n\n## P0 — Must Have (Core, launch blocker)\n\n### User Authentication\n\nEmail + password registration and login. Required to post or join any listing.\n\n### Create a Reading Listing\n\nA user creates a listing with:\n- **Book** — selected via Open Library API (title, author, cover art auto-populated)\n- **Preferred language** — language for discussion\n- **Reading pace** — e.g., \"1 chapter per week\", \"50 pages per day\"\n- **Start date** — when the group begins reading\n- **Meeting format** — voice calls, text chat, or mixed\n- **Max group size** — author sets the cap (e.g., 3–6 people)\n\n### Browse Listings\n\nSimple chronological list of open listings. No search or filters at this stage — just a scrollable feed of available reading groups to join.\n\n### Join a Listing\n\nAny authenticated user can join an open listing. First-come-first-served, no approval step. A user can be part of multiple reading groups simultaneously.\n\n### Group Formation\n\nWhen a listing reaches its max group size:\n- The listing becomes invisible to non-participants (effectively closed)\n- The author is prompted to share a Telegram group link with participants\n- The author manually creates the Telegram group and pastes the invite link\n\n### Notifications\n\nEmail + in-app notifications for:\n- **Each new sign-up** — listing author is notified when someone joins\n- **Group full** — all participants are notified when the group reaches its cap\n\n### User Profile (Basic)\n\n- Display name\n- Short bio\n\n## P1 — Should Have (Important, not launch blocker)\n\n### Search and Filters\n\n- Search listings by book title or author\n- Filter by reading pace, start date, meeting format\n- Browsable feed with sorting options\n\n### Telegram Group Creation Assistance\n\nProvide guidance or tooling to help the listing author create and share the Telegram group link more easily (e.g., step-by-step instructions, link validation).\n\n### Extended User Profile\n\n- Reading history (past groups participated in)\n- \"Currently reading\" list\n- Favorite genres\n\n## P2 — Nice to Have (Bonus, if time permits)\n\n### Automatic Telegram Group Creation\n\nSystem auto-creates a Telegram group via API and shares the invite link with all participants — no manual step needed.\n\n### Applicant Approval System\n\nListing author can review and approve/reject users who want to join, instead of first-come-first-served.\n\n### Rating and Reputation System\n\nUsers can rate their reading group experience. Build a reputation score based on participation history and peer ratings.\n\n### Discord Support\n\nAllow group creation via Discord as an alternative to Telegram.\n\n## Non-Goals\n\n- No payment or monetization features\n- No mobile app (web only)\n- No real-time chat within the platform (groups communicate via Telegram)\n- No complex third-party integrations beyond Open Library API\n- No AI-powered recommendations or matching (AI is allowed where it genuinely improves UX)\n- No content hosting (e-books, PDFs, etc.)\n\n## Tech Stack\n\n- **Frontend + Backend:** Next.js (full-stack)\n- **Database:** SQLite (local, persistent across container restarts)\n- **Analytics:** SQLite-based (no third-party analytics)\n- **Deployment:** Docker container with mounted volume for SQLite persistence\n- **Book data:** Open Library API (free, no API key required)\n","numLines":113,"startLine":1,"totalLines":113}}}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_013kg2NSR1dHZSda4hh24VXm","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Agent Memory\n     2→\n     3→## Project\n     4→- **Name**: Bookmate\n     5→- **Repo**: https://github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate\n     6→- **Project Board**: Bookmate (project #1 on TommyBrennan)\n     7→- **Tech**: Next.js 15, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4, SQLite, iron-session\n     8→- **Owner**: XanderZhu (human), TommyBrennan (GH account)\n     9→\n    10→## Current State\n    11→- Core MVP merged to main (PR #15)\n    12→- All 7 P0 issues (#1-#7) completed\n    13→- All 3 P1 issues completed: #8 (merged), #9 (merged), #10 (PR #18 open)\n    14→\n    15→## Open PRs\n    16→- #18: Extended User Profile (feat/extended-profile) — closes #10\n    17→\n    18→## Open Issues\n    19→- P1: #10 (Extended Profile — PR #18)\n    20→- P2: #11 (Telegram auto-create), #12 (Approval system), #13 (Ratings), #14 (Discord)\n    21→\n    22→## Important Notes\n    23→- GH_TOKEN loaded from `.claude-beat/.env`\n    24→- Remote URL includes PAT: reset with `git remote set-url` if needed\n    25→- Next.js 15 used (not 16) due to Turbopack /root permission issue\n    26→- agent-browser consistently crashes in this container (Chromium memory) — verify with curl instead\n    27→- SQLite DB stored in `app/data/` (gitignored)\n    28→- No production environment deployed yet\n    29→- DB has user_genres table added in feat/extended-profile branch\n    30→\n    31→## Next Session Priority\n    32→1. Merge PR #18 (1 session elapsed, no objections)\n    33→2. All P1 issues will be complete — consider deployment setup\n    34→3. Start P2 issues if time permits (#11-#14)\n    35→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"235c9f17-c672-4220-8162-6bda60e23e6c","uuid":"0721edb9-b203-432f-b78c-f4a43e29be3f","timestamp":"2026-03-19T23:00:13.705Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/memory/MEMORY.md","content":"# Agent Memory\n\n## Project\n- **Name**: Bookmate\n- **Repo**: https://github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate\n- **Project Board**: Bookmate (project #1 on TommyBrennan)\n- **Tech**: Next.js 15, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4, SQLite, iron-session\n- **Owner**: XanderZhu (human), TommyBrennan (GH account)\n\n## Current State\n- Core MVP merged to main (PR #15)\n- All 7 P0 issues (#1-#7) completed\n- All 3 P1 issues completed: #8 (merged), #9 (merged), #10 (PR #18 open)\n\n## Open PRs\n- #18: Extended User Profile (feat/extended-profile) — closes #10\n\n## Open Issues\n- P1: #10 (Extended Profile — PR #18)\n- P2: #11 (Telegram auto-create), #12 (Approval system), #13 (Ratings), #14 (Discord)\n\n## Important Notes\n- GH_TOKEN loaded from `.claude-beat/.env`\n- Remote URL includes PAT: reset with `git remote set-url` if needed\n- Next.js 15 used (not 16) due to Turbopack /root permission issue\n- agent-browser consistently crashes in this container (Chromium memory) — verify with curl instead\n- SQLite DB stored in `app/data/` (gitignored)\n- No production environment deployed yet\n- DB has user_genres table added in feat/extended-profile branch\n\n## Next Session Priority\n1. Merge PR #18 (1 session elapsed, no objections)\n2. All P1 issues will be complete — consider deployment setup\n3. Start P2 issues if time permits (#11-#14)\n","numLines":35,"startLine":1,"totalLines":35}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01EBzUtSzoKRV1YiSD2EhtqB","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Learned Patterns\n     2→\n     3→## GitHub Authentication\n     4→- Always export GH_TOKEN from .env before running gh commands:\n     5→  ```bash\n     6→  export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2)\n     7→  ```\n     8→- Cannot `--add-reviewer` when token owner is the PR author (HTTP 422)\n     9→- Repo created under TommyBrennan (not XanderZhu — can't create for other users)\n    10→\n    11→## Session Flow\n    12→- First session bootstraps repo, creates issues from PRD as `approved` + `prd`\n    13→- PRD features are pre-approved — no proposal step needed\n    14→- Focus on housekeeping when blocked: organize project board, update memory\n    15→\n    16→## Environment\n    17→- Find agent-browser with: `which agent-browser`\n    18→- agent-browser requires `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp` prefix due to socket dir permissions\n    19→- Playwright chromium needs manual install: `npx playwright install chromium`\n    20→- Git remote uses PAT in URL: `https://ghp_...@github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate.git`\n    21→- Git remote URL may lose PAT between sessions — re-set with `git remote set-url`\n    22→- Must set `git config user.email` and `user.name` each session if not set globally\n    23→\n    24→## Build\n    25→- Next.js 16 Turbopack fails in this environment (can't read /root dir)\n    26→- Use Next.js 15 which defaults to webpack\n    27→- Build from app/ directory: `cd app && npx next build`\n    28→- `useRef<T>()` needs explicit `undefined` arg in React 19: `useRef<T>(undefined)`\n    29→- better-sqlite3 `.all()` returns `unknown[]` — cast explicitly for TS\n    30→\n    31→## Browser Testing\n    32→- agent-browser crashes consistently in this container environment (\"Page crashed\")\n    33→- Workaround: use `curl` to verify HTML output and API responses\n    34→- For API testing: `curl -s URL | python3 -m json.tool`\n    35→\n    36→## Working Directory\n    37→- Project root: /root/Projects/bookmate\n    38→- App directory: /root/Projects/bookmate/app\n    39→- Always use full paths for .claude-beat/.env references\n    40→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"235c9f17-c672-4220-8162-6bda60e23e6c","uuid":"7831fa4e-c870-42c7-b02c-d12b94ff0f1c","timestamp":"2026-03-19T23:00:14.135Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md","content":"# Learned Patterns\n\n## GitHub Authentication\n- Always export GH_TOKEN from .env before running gh commands:\n  ```bash\n  export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2)\n  ```\n- Cannot `--add-reviewer` when token owner is the PR author (HTTP 422)\n- Repo created under TommyBrennan (not XanderZhu — can't create for other users)\n\n## Session Flow\n- First session bootstraps repo, creates issues from PRD as `approved` + `prd`\n- PRD features are pre-approved — no proposal step needed\n- Focus on housekeeping when blocked: organize project board, update memory\n\n## Environment\n- Find agent-browser with: `which agent-browser`\n- agent-browser requires `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp` prefix due to socket dir permissions\n- Playwright chromium needs manual install: `npx playwright install chromium`\n- Git remote uses PAT in URL: `https://ghp_...@github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate.git`\n- Git remote URL may lose PAT between sessions — re-set with `git remote set-url`\n- Must set `git config user.email` and `user.name` each session if not set globally\n\n## Build\n- Next.js 16 Turbopack fails in this environment (can't read /root dir)\n- Use Next.js 15 which defaults to webpack\n- Build from app/ directory: `cd app && npx next build`\n- `useRef<T>()` needs explicit `undefined` arg in React 19: `useRef<T>(undefined)`\n- better-sqlite3 `.all()` returns `unknown[]` — cast explicitly for TS\n\n## Browser Testing\n- agent-browser crashes consistently in this container environment (\"Page crashed\")\n- Workaround: use `curl` to verify HTML output and API responses\n- For API testing: `curl -s URL | python3 -m json.tool`\n\n## Working Directory\n- Project root: /root/Projects/bookmate\n- App directory: /root/Projects/bookmate/app\n- Always use full paths for .claude-beat/.env references\n","numLines":40,"startLine":1,"totalLines":40}}}

   38→{\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Base directory for this skill: /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude/skills/session-workflow\\n\\n# Session Workflow\\n\\nThis skill expands each step of the core loop: **READ → REVIEW → DECIDE → ACT → VERIFY → DOCUMENT**.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 1. READ — Load Context\\n\\n- Check if `REPOSITORY.md` exists. If not, invoke the `repo-bootstrap` skill before proceeding.\\n- Read `.claude-beat/PRD.md`, `.claude-beat/memory/MEMORY.md`, `.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md`\\n- If PRD.md references additional files, read those too\\n- Read the last 2–3 session logs from `.claude-beat/logs/sessions/`\\n- Fetch open issues and PRs via `gh` CLI\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 2. REVIEW — Assess Current State\\n\\nBefore picking new work, check what needs attention right now.\\n\\n### 2a. Production Health Check (every session)\\n\\nIf the project has a production or staging environment:\\n\\n1. Identify the production URL (from memory, `.env`, deploy config, or README). If no production URL can be found, skip the production health check and note \\\"No production environment detected\\\" in the session log.\\n2. Use `which agent-browser` to locate the browser tool. Always attempt browser verification before falling back to non-visual checks. Open the production URL and verify core functionality works.\\n3. Check for visible errors, broken pages, or degraded behavior\\n4. If the platform provides logs or monitoring (Render, Vercel, etc.) — check recent error logs\\n\\n**If production issues are found:**\\n- **Critical** (app down, core flow broken, data loss risk): fix immediately in this session, create a PR, deploy\\n- **Medium** (non-blocking bugs, UI glitches, non-critical errors): create a GitHub issue labeled `approved`, `bug` with priority `P1`\\n- **Low** (cosmetic, minor warnings): create a GitHub issue labeled `approved`, `bug` with priority `P2`\\n\\n### 2b. Follow Up on Previous Sessions\\n\\nRead the most recent session logs and check for:\\n\\n- **Deployments**: if something was deployed last session, verify it is live and working (part of 2a)\\n- **Human action requests**: if a previous session created a `needs-human` or `blocked` issue, check whether the human has acted on it\\n  - If resolved — acknowledge, close the issue, continue with any unblocked work\\n  - If stale (no response for 2+ sessions) — add a comment on the issue reminding the owner, and note it in the session log\\n- **Open PRs with review comments**: if a PR has unaddressed reviewer feedback, respond and update before opening new PRs\\n- **WIP branches**: if the previous session left work-in-progress, pick it up and finish it\\n\\n### 2c. PRD Sync\\n\\nScan PRD (and any referenced spec files) for features with no corresponding GitHub issue. For each missing feature:\\n\\n- Label: `approved` + `prd` + priority (`P0`/`P1`/`P2` matching the PRD)\\n- Title prefix: `prd:` (e.g., `prd: add CEFR level selector`)\\n- These are pre-approved — no proposal step, no waiting for human reaction\\n\\nSearch existing issues first to avoid duplicates (`gh issue list --state all`). This step runs every session regardless of other work.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 3. DECIDE — Pick What to Work On\\n\\nWrite a brief triage summary in your session log before starting work:\\n- Production status (healthy / issues found)\\n- Follow-ups resolved or escalated\\n- What you will work on this session and why\\n\\nSelect work in this priority order:\\n\\n1. **Critical production issues** found during REVIEW\\n2. **Approved `P0` issues** — launch blockers, critical bugs, critical PRD features\\n3. **Open PRs needing attention** — review comments, merge-ready PRs, CI failures\\n4. **In-progress work** from previous sessions (WIP branches, draft PRs)\\n5. **Approved `P1` and `P2` issues** — P1 first, then P2\\n6. **Stale proposals** — if a `proposal` issue has had no human reaction for 2+ sessions, change label to `approved` and comment \\\"Auto-approving after no objection.\\\" Never self-approve issues labeled `needs-human` or `blocked`.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 4. ACT — Execute\\n\\nFor each piece of work:\\n1. Implement the change\\n2. Run lint, tests, build\\n3. Test using `agent-browser` skill if UI-related\\n4. Commit and create/update PR (use `pr-lifecycle` skill)\\n5. If the primary item finishes quickly and there is a small, self-contained second item, you may take it on. Do not start a third item — leave it for the next session.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 5. VERIFY — Post-Deploy Check\\n\\nIf you deployed anything this session:\\n1. Verify the deployment succeeded\\n2. If browser testing tooling is available, open production and confirm the change is live\\n3. Check for regressions — test core flows, not just the new feature\\n\\nIf you opened or updated a PR this session:\\n- Verify CI checks are passing before closing the session\\n\\nIf you did NOT deploy but production had issues in REVIEW:\\n- Confirm your fix PR is open and ready for review\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 6. DOCUMENT — Close the Session\\n\\n1. Write session log to `.claude-beat/logs/sessions/YYYY-MM-DD_HH-MM.md`\\n2. Update `.claude-beat/memory/MEMORY.md`\\n3. Update `.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md` if you learned something new\\n4. Ensure no uncommitted changes remain (`git status`)\\n5. All branches pushed, all work has a PR (draft is fine for WIP)\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Quick Reference: Session Checklist\\n\\n```\\nREAD\\n[ ] Context loaded (PRD, memory, logs, issues, PRs)\\n\\nREVIEW\\n[ ] Production health checked\\n[ ] Previous session follow-ups addressed\\n\\nDECIDE\\n[ ] Triage summary written\\n[ ] Work selected by priority\\n\\nACT\\n[ ] Changes implemented, tested, PR opened\\n\\nVERIFY\\n[ ] CI checks passing (if PR opened)\\n[ ] Production check (if deployed)\\n\\nDOCUMENT\\n[ ] Session log written\\n[ ] Memory updated\\n[ ] No uncommitted changes\\n```\\n\"}]},\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"session_id\":\"235c9f17-c672-4220-8162-6bda60e23e6c\",\"uuid\":\"eac833db-d209-4182-ad66-5d5b99519dc1\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-03-19T23:00:07.982Z\",\"isSynthetic\":true}\n session-workflow\"}]},\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"session_id\":\"235c9f17-c672-4220-8162-6bda60e23e6c\",\"uuid\":\"6914d34f-b059-4e73-a7df-705d7a8b09ff\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-03-19T23:00:07.983Z\",\"tool_use_result\":{\"success\":true,\"commandName\":\"session-workflow\"}}\n{\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Base directory for this skill: /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude/skills/session-workflow\\n\\n# Session Workflow\\n\\nThis skill expands each step of the core loop: **READ → REVIEW → DECIDE → ACT → VERIFY → DOCUMENT**.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 1. READ — Load Context\\n\\n- Check if `REPOSITORY.md` exists. If not, invoke the `repo-bootstrap` skill before proceeding.\\n- Read `.claude-beat/PRD.md`, `.claude-beat/memory/MEMORY.md`, `.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md`\\n- If PRD.md references additional files, read those too\\n- Read the last 2–3 session logs from `.claude-beat/logs/sessions/`\\n- Fetch open issues and PRs via `gh` CLI\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 2. REVIEW — Assess Current State\\n\\nBefore picking new work, check what needs attention right now.\\n\\n### 2a. Production Health Check (every session)\\n\\nIf the project has a production or staging environment:\\n\\n1. Identify the production URL (from memory, `.env`, deploy config, or README). If no production URL can be found, skip the production health check and note \\\"No production environment detected\\\" in the session log.\\n2. Use `which agent-browser` to locate the browser tool. Always attempt browser verification before falling back to non-visual checks. Open the production URL and verify core functionality works.\\n3. Check for visible errors, broken pages, or degraded behavior\\n4. If the platform provides logs or monitoring (Render, Vercel, etc.) — check recent error logs\\n\\n**If production issues are found:**\\n- **Critical** (app down, core flow broken, data loss risk): fix immediately in this session, create a PR, deploy\\n- **Medium** (non-blocking bugs, UI glitches, non-critical errors): create a GitHub issue labeled `approved`, `bug` with priority `P1`\\n- **Low** (cosmetic, minor warnings): create a GitHub issue labeled `approved`, `bug` with priority `P2`\\n\\n### 2b. Follow Up on Previous Sessions\\n\\nRead the most recent session logs and check for:\\n\\n- **Deployments**: if something was deployed last session, verify it is live and working (part of 2a)\\n- **Human action requests**: if a previous session created a `needs-human` or `blocked` issue, check whether the human has acted on it\\n  - If resolved — acknowledge, close the issue, continue with any unblocked work\\n  - If stale (no response for 2+ sessions) — add a comment on the issue reminding the owner, and note it in the session log\\n- **Open PRs with review comments**: if a PR has unaddressed reviewer feedback, respond and update before opening new PRs\\n- **WIP branches**: if the previous session left work-in-progress, pick it up and finish it\\n\\n### 2c. PRD Sync\\n\\nScan PRD (and any referenced spec files) for features with no corresponding GitHub issue. For each missing feature:\\n\\n- Label: `approved` + `prd` + priority (`P0`/`P1`/`P2` matching the PRD)\\n- Title prefix: `prd:` (e.g., `prd: add CEFR level selector`)\\n- These are pre-approved — no proposal step, no waiting for human reaction\\n\\nSearch existing issues first to avoid duplicates (`gh issue list --state all`). This step runs every session regardless of other work.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 3. DECIDE — Pick What to Work On\\n\\nWrite a brief triage summary in your session log before starting work:\\n- Production status (healthy / issues found)\\n- Follow-ups resolved or escalated\\n- What you will work on this session and why\\n\\nSelect work in this priority order:\\n\\n1. **Critical production issues** found during REVIEW\\n2. **Approved `P0` issues** — launch blockers, critical bugs, critical PRD features\\n3. **Open PRs needing attention** — review comments, merge-ready PRs, CI failures\\n4. **In-progress work** from previous sessions (WIP branches, draft PRs)\\n5. **Approved `P1` and `P2` issues** — P1 first, then P2\\n6. **Stale proposals** — if a `proposal` issue has had no human reaction for 2+ sessions, change label to `approved` and comment \\\"Auto-approving after no objection.\\\" Never self-approve issues labeled `needs-human` or `blocked`.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 4. ACT — Execute\\n\\nFor each piece of work:\\n1. Implement the change\\n2. Run lint, tests, build\\n3. Test using `agent-browser` skill if UI-related\\n4. Commit and create/update PR (use `pr-lifecycle` skill)\\n5. If the primary item finishes quickly and there is a small, self-contained second item, you may take it on. Do not start a third item — leave it for the next session.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 5. VERIFY — Post-Deploy Check\\n\\nIf you deployed anything this session:\\n1. Verify the deployment succeeded\\n2. If browser testing tooling is available, open production and confirm the change is live\\n3. Check for regressions — test core flows, not just the new feature\\n\\nIf you opened or updated a PR this session:\\n- Verify CI checks are passing before closing the session\\n\\nIf you did NOT deploy but production had issues in REVIEW:\\n- Confirm your fix PR is open and ready for review\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 6. DOCUMENT — Close the Session\\n\\n1. Write session log to `.claude-beat/logs/sessions/YYYY-MM-DD_HH-MM.md`\\n2. Update `.claude-beat/memory/MEMORY.md`\\n3. Update `.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md` if you learned something new\\n4. Ensure no uncommitted changes remain (`git status`)\\n5. All branches pushed, all work has a PR (draft is fine for WIP)\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Quick Reference: Session Checklist\\n\\n```\\nREAD\\n[ ] Context loaded (PRD, memory, logs, issues, PRs)\\n\\nREVIEW\\n[ ] Production health checked\\n[ ] Previous session follow-ups addressed\\n\\nDECIDE\\n[ ] Triage summary written\\n[ ] Work selected by priority\\n\\nACT\\n[ ] Changes implemented, tested, PR opened\\n\\nVERIFY\\n[ ] CI checks passing (if PR opened)\\n[ ] Production check (if deployed)\\n\\nDOCUMENT\\n[ ] Session log written\\n[ ] Memory updated\\n[ ] No uncommitted changes\\n```\\n\"}]},\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"session_id\":\"235c9f17-c672-4220-8162-6bda60e23e6c\",\"uuid\":\"eac833db-d209-4182-ad66-5d5b99519dc1\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-03-19T23:00:07.982Z\",\"isSynthetic\":true}\n{\"type\":\"stream_event\",\"event\":{\"type\":\"message_stop\"},\"session_id\":\"235c9f17-c672-4220-8162-6bda60e23e6c\",\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"uuid\":\"f5bf7485-5b93-4808-8dba-d584edd8ecdc\"}\n{\"type\":\"rate_limit_event\",\"rate_limit_info\":{\"status\":\"allowed\",\"resetsAt\":1773968400,\"rateLimitType\":\"five_hour\",\"overageStatus\":\"rejected\",\"overageDisabledReason\":\"out_of_credits\",\"isUsingOverage\":false},\"uuid\":\"dec4ebe1-3008-4604-aab9-c42a03aaf2ae\",\"session_id\":\"235c9f17-c672-4220-8162-6bda60e23e6c\"}\n{\"type\":\"stream_event\",\"event\":{\"type\":\"message_start\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-opus-4-6\",\"id\":\"msg_01YYRaheymYeYTQF8EZPTdUy\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":2,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":1620,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":13562,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":1620},\"output_tokens\":10,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}}},\"session_id\":\"235c9f17-c672-4220-8162-6bda60e23e6c\",\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"uuid\":\"62723f22-722f-4fa8-9254-32505cea9d77\"}\n{\"type\":\"stream_event\",\"event\":{\"type\":\"content_block_start\",\"index\":0,\"content_block\":{\"type\":\"thinking\",\"thinking\":\"\",\"signature\":\"\"}},\"session_id\":\"235c9f17-c672-4220-8162-6bda60e23e6c\",\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"uuid\":\"c51abb8c-fe67-4e38-92b5-8fd24a193f4f\"}\n{\"type\":\"stream_event\",\"event\":{\"type\":\"content_block_delta\",\"index\":0,\"delta\":{\"type\":\"thinking_delta\",\"thinking\":\"Let {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01M4BbbA9kGwHYfKM8fnCe8m","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01H6FkJUTjDNxPi6WXA9M3n8","name":"Agent","input":{"subagent_type":"feature-dev:code-architect","description":"Design approval system architecture","prompt":"I need to implement an Applicant Approval System for the Bookmate app (Next.js 15, App Router, SQLite via better-sqlite3, iron-session auth).\n\nCurrent behavior: When users join a listing, it's first-come-first-served (auto-join). Listings auto-close when member count reaches max_group_size.\n\nNew behavior: Listing authors can optionally enable an approval system when creating a listing. When enabled:\n- Users \"apply\" instead of directly joining\n- The listing author sees pending applicants and can approve/reject each\n- Approved users become members\n- Rejected users are notified\n- The listing closes when approved members reach max_group_size\n\nPlease analyze the existing codebase and design the implementation:\n1. Read the DB schema in app/lib/db.ts\n2. Read the listing creation page at app/app/listings/create/page.tsx\n3. Read the listing detail page at app/app/listings/[id]/page.tsx\n4. Read the listing API routes at app/app/api/listings/\n5. Read the notifications system at app/lib/notifications.ts and app/app/api/notifications/\n\nProvide:\n- DB schema changes needed (new columns, new tables)\n- API endpoint changes/additions\n- UI changes needed\n- Implementation sequence"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":297,"cache_read_input_tokens":30814,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":297},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"235c9f17-c672-4220-8162-6bda60e23e6c","uuid":"00d5cb7c-ea25-46e7-878b-0b4cdf692229"}
{"type":"system","subtype":"task_started","task_id":"abf4692fecf04c5cd","tool_use_id":"toolu_01H6FkJUTjDNxPi6WXA9M3n8","description":"Design approval system architecture","task_type":"local_agent","prompt":"I need to implement an Applicant Approval System for the Bookmate app (Next.js 15, App Router, SQLite via better-sqlite3, iron-session auth).\n\nCurrent behavior: When users join a listing, it's first-come-first-served (auto-join). Listings auto-close when member count reaches max_group_size.\n\nNew behavior: Listing authors can optionally enable an approval system when creating a listing. When enabled:\n- Users \"apply\" instead of directly joining\n- The listing author sees pending applicants and can approve/reject each\n- Approved users become members\n- Rejected users are notified\n- The listing closes when approved members reach max_group_size\n\nPlease analyze the existing codebase and design the implementation:\n1. Read the DB schema in app/lib/db.ts\n2. Read the listing creation page at app/app/listings/create/page.tsx\n3. Read the listing detail page at app/app/listings/[id]/page.tsx\n4. Read the listing API routes at app/app/api/listings/\n5. Read the notifications system at app/lib/notifications.ts and app/app/api/notifications/\n\nProvide:\n- DB schema changes needed (new columns, new tables)\n- API endpoint changes/additions\n- UI changes needed\n- Implementation sequence","uuid":"5f0b3738-bb17-40da-a438-e5056c0d4e5a","session_id":"235c9f17-c672-4220-8162-6bda60e23e6c"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"I need to implement an Applicant Approval System for the Bookmate app (Next.js 15, App Router, SQLite via better-sqlite3, iron-session auth).\n\nCurrent behavior: When users join a listing, it's first-come-first-served (auto-join). Listings auto-close when member count reaches max_group_size.\n\nNew behavior: Listing authors can optionally enable an approval system when creating a listing. When enabled:\n- Users \"apply\" instead of directly joining\n- The listing author sees pending applicants and can approve/reject each\n- Approved users become members\n- Rejected users are notified\n- The listing closes when approved members reach max_group_size\n\nPlease analyze the existing codebase and design the implementation:\n1. Read the DB schema in app/lib/db.ts\n2. Read the listing creation page at app/app/listings/create/page.tsx\n3. Read the listing detail page at app/app/listings/[id]/page.tsx\n4. Read the listing API routes at app/app/api/listings/\n5. Read the notifications system at app/lib/notifications.ts and app/app/api/notifications/\n\nProvide:\n- DB schema changes needed (new columns, new tables)\n- API endpoint changes/additions\n- UI changes needed\n- Implementation sequence"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01H6FkJUTjDNxPi6WXA9M3n8","session_id":"235c9f17-c672-4220-8162-6bda60e23e6c","uuid":"e3c6e813-388a-42d4-976b-9589534bf08e","timestamp":"2026-03-19T23:01:16.883Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01RfrnRxdP2NKNUaF2BL7FNF","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import Database from \"better-sqlite3\";\n     2→import path from \"path\";\n     3→import fs from \"fs\";\n     4→\n     5→const DB_DIR = process.env.DB_DIR || path.join(process.cwd(), \"data\");\n     6→const DB_PATH = path.join(DB_DIR, \"bookmate.db\");\n     7→\n     8→// Ensure data directory exists\n     9→if (!fs.existsSync(DB_DIR)) {\n    10→  fs.mkdirSync(DB_DIR, { recursive: true });\n    11→}\n    12→\n    13→const db = new Database(DB_PATH);\n    14→\n    15→// Enable WAL mode for better concurrent performance\n    16→db.pragma(\"journal_mode = WAL\");\n    17→db.pragma(\"foreign_keys = ON\");\n    18→\n    19→// Initialize schema\n    20→db.exec(`\n    21→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (\n    22→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    23→    email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,\n    24→    password_hash TEXT NOT NULL,\n    25→    display_name TEXT NOT NULL,\n    26→    bio TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    27→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))\n    28→  );\n    29→\n    30→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listings (\n    31→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    32→    author_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    33→    book_title TEXT NOT NULL,\n    34→    book_author TEXT NOT NULL,\n    35→    book_cover_url TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    36→    book_olid TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    37→    language TEXT DEFAULT 'English',\n    38→    reading_pace TEXT NOT NULL,\n    39→    start_date TEXT NOT NULL,\n    40→    meeting_format TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(meeting_format IN ('voice', 'text', 'mixed')),\n    41→    max_group_size INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK(max_group_size >= 2 AND max_group_size <= 20),\n    42→    telegram_link TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    43→    is_full INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n    44→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    45→    FOREIGN KEY (author_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    46→  );\n    47→\n    48→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listing_members (\n    49→    listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    50→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    51→    joined_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    52→    PRIMARY KEY (listing_id, user_id),\n    53→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id),\n    54→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    55→  );\n    56→\n    57→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS user_genres (\n    58→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    59→    genre TEXT NOT NULL,\n    60→    PRIMARY KEY (user_id, genre),\n    61→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    62→  );\n    63→\n    64→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS notifications (\n    65→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    66→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    67→    listing_id INTEGER,\n    68→    type TEXT NOT NULL,\n    69→    message TEXT NOT NULL,\n    70→    is_read INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n    71→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    72→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id),\n    73→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id)\n    74→  );\n    75→`);\n    76→\n    77→export default db;\n    78→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01H6FkJUTjDNxPi6WXA9M3n8","session_id":"235c9f17-c672-4220-8162-6bda60e23e6c","uuid":"3cacd2e6-9f19-4be0-a531-a267010c6da5","timestamp":"2026-03-19T23:01:19.319Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_016crAjDQodvVVTJUQtMqgMY","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→\"use client\";\n     2→\n     3→import { useState } from \"react\";\n     4→import { useRouter } from \"next/navigation\";\n     5→import BookSearch from \"@/components/BookSearch\";\n     6→\n     7→export default function CreateListingPage() {\n     8→  const router = useRouter();\n     9→  const [error, setError] = useState(\"\");\n    10→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false);\n    11→\n    12→  const [bookTitle, setBookTitle] = useState(\"\");\n    13→  const [bookAuthor, setBookAuthor] = useState(\"\");\n    14→  const [bookCoverUrl, setBookCoverUrl] = useState(\"\");\n    15→  const [bookOlid, setBookOlid] = useState(\"\");\n    16→  const [language, setLanguage] = useState(\"English\");\n    17→  const [readingPace, setReadingPace] = useState(\"\");\n    18→  const [startDate, setStartDate] = useState(\"\");\n    19→  const [meetingFormat, setMeetingFormat] = useState(\"text\");\n    20→  const [maxGroupSize, setMaxGroupSize] = useState(\"4\");\n    21→\n    22→  const handleBookSelect = (book: {\n    23→    title: string;\n    24→    author: string;\n    25→    coverUrl: string;\n    26→    olid: string;\n    27→  }) => {\n    28→    setBookTitle(book.title);\n    29→    setBookAuthor(book.author);\n    30→    setBookCoverUrl(book.coverUrl);\n    31→    setBookOlid(book.olid);\n    32→  };\n    33→\n    34→  const handleSubmit = async (e: React.FormEvent) => {\n    35→    e.preventDefault();\n    36→    setError(\"\");\n    37→    setLoading(true);\n    38→\n    39→    try {\n    40→      const res = await fetch(\"/api/listings/create\", {\n    41→        method: \"POST\",\n    42→        headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n    43→        body: JSON.stringify({\n    44→          bookTitle,\n    45→          bookAuthor,\n    46→          bookCoverUrl,\n    47→          bookOlid,\n    48→          language,\n    49→          readingPace,\n    50→          startDate,\n    51→          meetingFormat,\n    52→          maxGroupSize,\n    53→        }),\n    54→      });\n    55→\n    56→      const data = await res.json();\n    57→\n    58→      if (!res.ok) {\n    59→        if (res.status === 401) {\n    60→          router.push(\"/auth/login\");\n    61→          return;\n    62→        }\n    63→        setError(data.error || \"Failed to create listing\");\n    64→        return;\n    65→      }\n    66→\n    67→      router.push(`/listings/${data.id}`);\n    68→    } catch {\n    69→      setError(\"Something went wrong. Please try again.\");\n    70→    } finally {\n    71→      setLoading(false);\n    72→    }\n    73→  };\n    74→\n    75→  return (\n    76→    <div className=\"max-w-lg mx-auto\">\n    77→      <h1 className=\"text-3xl mb-2\">Start a reading group</h1>\n    78→      <p\n    79→        className=\"mb-8\"\n    80→        style={{\n    81→          color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n    82→          fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\",\n    83→          fontSize: \"0.95rem\",\n    84→        }}\n    85→      >\n    86→        Choose a book, set your pace, and wait for readers to join.\n    87→      </p>\n    88→\n    89→      {error && (\n    90→        <div\n    91→          className=\"mb-4 p-3 rounded-lg text-sm\"\n    92→          style={{\n    93→            backgroundColor: \"rgba(192, 57, 43, 0.08)\",\n    94→            color: \"var(--color-error)\",\n    95→            fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\",\n    96→          }}\n    97→        >\n    98→          {error}\n    99→        </div>\n   100→      )}\n   101→\n   102→      <form onSubmit={handleSubmit} className=\"space-y-5\">\n   103→        <div>\n   104→          <label\n   105→            className=\"block text-sm font-semibold mb-1\"\n   106→            style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n   107→          >\n   108→            Book\n   109→          </label>\n   110→          <BookSearch onSelect={handleBookSelect} />\n   111→          {bookTitle && (\n   112→            <div\n   113→              className=\"mt-2 flex items-center gap-3 p-3 rounded-lg\"\n   114→              style={{ backgroundColor: \"rgba(224, 122, 58, 0.06)\" }}\n   115→            >\n   116→              {bookCoverUrl && (\n   117→                <img\n   118→                  src={bookCoverUrl}\n   119→                  alt=\"\"\n   120→                  className=\"w-10 h-14 object-cover rounded\"\n   121→                />\n   122→              )}\n   123→              <div>\n   124→                <div\n   125→                  className=\"text-sm font-semibold\"\n   126→                  style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n   127→                >\n   128→                  {bookTitle}\n   129→                </div>\n   130→                <div\n   131→                  className=\"text-xs\"\n   132→                  style={{\n   133→                    color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n   134→                    fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\",\n   135→                  }}\n   136→                >\n   137→                  by {bookAuthor}\n   138→                </div>\n   139→              </div>\n   140→            </div>\n   141→          )}\n   142→        </div>\n   143→\n   144→        <div>\n   145→          <label\n   146→            className=\"block text-sm font-semibold mb-1\"\n   147→            style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n   148→          >\n   149→            Discussion language\n   150→          </label>\n   151→          <select\n   152→            className=\"input-field\"\n   153→            value={language}\n   154→            onChange={(e) => setLanguage(e.target.value)}\n   155→          >\n   156→            <option>English</option>\n   157→            <option>Spanish</option>\n   158→            <option>French</option>\n   159→            <option>German</option>\n   160→            <option>Russian</option>\n   161→            <option>Portuguese</option>\n   162→            <option>Italian</option>\n   163→            <option>Japanese</option>\n   164→            <option>Chinese</option>\n   165→            <option>Korean</option>\n   166→            <option>Other</option>\n   167→          </select>\n   168→        </div>\n   169→\n   170→        <div>\n   171→          <label\n   172→            className=\"block text-sm font-semibold mb-1\"\n   173→            style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n   174→          >\n   175→            Reading pace\n   176→          </label>\n   177→          <input\n   178→            type=\"text\"\n   179→            className=\"input-field\"\n   180→            value={readingPace}\n   181→            onChange={(e) => setReadingPace(e.target.value)}\n   182→            required\n   183→            placeholder='e.g. \"1 chapter per week\" or \"50 pages per day\"'\n   184→          />\n   185→        </div>\n   186→\n   187→        <div>\n   188→          <label\n   189→            className=\"block text-sm font-semibold mb-1\"\n   190→            style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n   191→          >\n   192→            Start date\n   193→          </label>\n   194→          <input\n   195→            type=\"date\"\n   196→            className=\"input-field\"\n   197→            value={startDate}\n   198→            onChange={(e) => setStartDate(e.target.value)}\n   199→            required\n   200→          />\n   201→        </div>\n   202→\n   203→        <div>\n   204→          <label\n   205→            className=\"block text-sm font-semibold mb-1\"\n   206→            style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n   207→          >\n   208→            Meeting format\n   209→          </label>\n   210→          <div className=\"flex gap-3\">\n   211→            {[\n   212→              { value: \"text\", label: \"Text chat\" },\n   213→              { value: \"voice\", label: \"Voice calls\" },\n   214→              { value: \"mixed\", label: \"Mixed\" },\n   215→            ].map((opt) => (\n   216→              <button\n   217→                key={opt.value}\n   218→                type=\"button\"\n   219→                onClick={() => setMeetingFormat(opt.value)}\n   220→                className=\"flex-1 py-2 px-3 rounded-lg text-sm font-semibold transition-colors cursor-pointer\"\n   221→                style={{\n   222→                  fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\",\n   223→                  backgroundColor:\n   224→                    meetingFormat === opt.value\n   225→                      ? \"var(--color-accent)\"\n   226→                      : \"transparent\",\n   227→                  color:\n   228→                    meetingFormat === opt.value\n   229→                      ? \"white\"\n   230→                      : \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n   231→                  border:\n   232→                    meetingFormat === opt.value\n   233→                      ? \"1.5px solid var(--color-accent)\"\n   234→                      : \"1.5px solid var(--color-border)\",\n   235→                }}\n   236→              >\n   237→                {opt.label}\n   238→              </button>\n   239→            ))}\n   240→          </div>\n   241→        </div>\n   242→\n   243→        <div>\n   244→          <label\n   245→            className=\"block text-sm font-semibold mb-1\"\n   246→            style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n   247→          >\n   248→            Maximum group size\n   249→          </label>\n   250→          <select\n   251→            className=\"input-field\"\n   252→            value={maxGroupSize}\n   253→            onChange={(e) => setMaxGroupSize(e.target.value)}\n   254→          >\n   255→            {[2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 15, 20].map((n) => (\n   256→              <option key={n} value={n}>\n   257→                {n} readers\n   258→              </option>\n   259→            ))}\n   260→          </select>\n   261→        </div>\n   262→\n   263→        <button\n   264→          type=\"submit\"\n   265→          className=\"btn-primary w-full\"\n   266→          disabled={loading || !bookTitle}\n   267→        >\n   268→          {loading ? \"Creating...\" : \"Create reading group\"}\n   269→        </button>\n   270→      </form>\n   271→    </div>\n   272→  );\n   273→}\n {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01Fa5njNRgrytm71AxUGFcUY","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import db from \"./db\";\n     2→\n     3→export function createNotification(\n     4→  userId: number,\n     5→  listingId: number,\n     6→  type: string,\n     7→  message: string\n     8→) {\n     9→  db.prepare(\n    10→    \"INSERT INTO notifications (user_id, listing_id, type, message) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)\"\n    11→  ).run(userId, listingId, type, message);\n    12→}\n    13→\n    14→export function notifyListingAuthor(\n    15→  listingId: number,\n    16→  joinerName: string\n    17→) {\n    18→  const listing = db\n    19→    .prepare(\n    20→      \"SELECT l.author_id, l.book_title FROM listings l WHERE l.id = ?\"\n    21→    )\n    22→    .get(listingId) as { author_id: number; book_title: string } | undefined;\n    23→\n    24→  if (listing) {\n    25→    createNotification(\n    26→      listing.author_id,\n    27→      listingId,\n    28→      \"new_member\",\n    29→      `${joinerName} joined your reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\"`\n    30→    );\n    31→  }\n    32→}\n    33→\n    34→export function notifyGroupFull(listingId: number) {\n    35→  const listing = db\n    36→    .prepare(\"SELECT book_title FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    37→    .get(listingId) as { book_title: string } | undefined;\n    38→  const members = db\n    39→    .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n    40→    .all(listingId) as { user_id: number }[];\n    41→\n    42→  if (listing) {\n    43→    for (const member of members) {\n    44→      createNotification(\n    45→        member.user_id,\n    46→        listingId,\n    47→        \"group_full\",\n    48→        `The reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" is now full! The organizer will share a Telegram link soon.`\n    49→      );\n    50→    }\n    51→  }\n    52→}\n    53→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01H6FkJUTjDNxPi6WXA9M3n8","session_id":"235c9f17-c672-4220-8162-6bda60e23e6c","uuid":"f6918464-701b-4fae-ab1c-2e78ccf9a12f","timestamp":"2026-03-19T23:01:20.300Z"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_0139yccuNwXF9Ka9e452qiWg","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01HevLD5WihgZCPtL3k1tc3h","name":"Read","input":{"file_path":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":354,"cache_read_input_tokens":18740,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":354,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":22,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01H6FkJUTjDNxPi6WXA9M3n8","session_id":"235c9f17-c672-4220-8162-6bda60e23e6c","uuid":"a6ff58a4-e02a-496d-92af-dc405631d13b"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01HevLD5WihgZCPtL3k1tc3h","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { notifyListingAuthor, notifyGroupFull } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n     5→\n     6→export async function POST(\n     7→  _req: NextRequest,\n     8→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n     9→) {\n    10→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    11→  if (!session) {\n    12→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    13→  }\n    14→\n    15→  const { id } = await params;\n    16→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    17→\n    18→  const listing = db\n    19→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    20→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    21→\n    22→  if (!listing) {\n    23→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    24→  }\n    25→\n    26→  if (listing.is_full) {\n    27→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"This group is already full\" }, { status: 400 });\n    28→  }\n    29→\n    30→  // Check if already a member\n    31→  const existing = db\n    32→    .prepare(\n    33→      \"SELECT 1 FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ?\"\n    34→    )\n    35→    .get(listingId, session.userId);\n    36→\n    37→  if (existing) {\n    38→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"You are already a member\" }, { status: 400 });\n    39→  }\n    40→\n    41→  // Count current members\n    42→  const { count } = db\n    43→    .prepare(\"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n    44→    .get(listingId) as { count: number };\n    45→\n    46→  if (count >= (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n    47→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"This group is already full\" }, { status: 400 });\n    48→  }\n    49→\n    50→  // Join\n    51→  db.prepare(\n    52→    \"INSERT INTO listing_members (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\"\n    53→  ).run(listingId, session.userId);\n    54→\n    55→  // Notify the author\n    56→  notifyListingAuthor(listingId, session.displayName || \"Someone\");\n    57→\n    58→  // Check if group is now full\n    59→  const newCount = count + 1;\n    60→  if (newCount >= (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n    61→    db.prepare(\"UPDATE listings SET is_full = 1 WHERE id = ?\").run(listingId);\n    62→    notifyGroupFull(listingId);\n    63→  }\n    64→\n    65→  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true, memberCount: newCount });\n    66→}\n    67→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01H6FkJUTjDNxPi6WXA9M3n8","session_id":"235c9f17-c672-4220-8162-6bda60e23e6c","uuid":"401b7a28-5c74-4b88-8fbd-8e1c468b875c","timestamp":"2026-03-19T23:01:28.642Z"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_0139yccuNwXF9Ka9e452qiWg","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_013DUvQ4Ld9KWkPdzsNY6TFw","name":"Read","input":{"file_path":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":354,"cache_read_input_tokens":18740,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":354,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":22,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01H6FkJUTjDNxPi6WXA9M3n8","session_id":"235c9f17-c672-4220-8162-6bda60e23e6c","uuid":"9bba9bf3-b382-43c5-ae08-58d64f357d53"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_013DUvQ4Ld9KWkPdzsNY6TFw","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { getSession } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→\n     5→export async function GET(\n     6→  _req: NextRequest,\n     7→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n     8→) {\n     9→  const { id } = await params;\n    10→\n    11→  const listing = db\n    12→    .prepare(\n    13→      `SELECT\n    14→        l.*,\n    15→        u.display_name as author_name\n    16→      FROM listings l\n    17→      JOIN users u ON l.author_id = u.id\n    18→      WHERE l.id = ?`\n    19→    )\n    20→    .get(id) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    21→\n    22→  if (!listing) {\n    23→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    24→  }\n    25→\n    26→  const members = db\n    27→    .prepare(\n    28→      `SELECT u.id, u.display_name, u.bio, lm.joined_at\n    29→       FROM listing_members lm\n    30→       JOIN users u ON lm.user_id = u.id\n    31→       WHERE lm.listing_id = ?\n    32→       ORDER BY lm.joined_at ASC`\n    33→    )\n    34→    .all(id) as { id: number; display_name: string; bio: string; joined_at: string }[];\n    35→\n    36→  const session = await getSession();\n    37→  const isMember = session.userId\n    38→    ? members.some((m) => m.id === session.userId)\n    39→    : false;\n    40→  const isAuthor = session.userId\n    41→    ? listing.author_id === session.userId\n    42→    : false;\n    43→\n    44→  return NextResponse.json({\n    45→    listing: {\n    46→      ...listing,\n    47→      members,\n    48→      memberCount: members.length,\n    49→      isMember,\n    50→      isAuthor,\n    51→    },\n    52→  });\n    53→}\n    54→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01H6FkJUTjDNxPi6WXA9M3n8","session_id":"235c9f17-c672-4220-8162-6bda60e23e6c","uuid":"940756da-0550-4650-9c2e-33dec734b23a","timestamp":"2026-03-19T23:01:28.975Z"}
{"type":"system","subtype":"task_progress","task_id":"abf4692fecf04c5cd","tool_use_id":"toolu_01H6FkJUTjDNxPi6WXA9M3n8","description":"Reading app/app/api/listings/create/route.ts","usage":{"total_tokens":19189,"tool_uses":9,"duration_ms":12487},"last_tool_name":"Read","uuid":"58f2c1ed-5dac-49b5-8772-e476ff360f5e","session_id":"235c9f17-c672-4220-8162-6bda60e23e6c"}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_0139yccuNwXF9Ka9e452qiWg","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01CY2J81Z9tkV4Z6iGbB2dAg","name":"Read","input":{"file_path":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/create/route.ts"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":354,"cache_read_input_tokens":18740,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":354,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":22,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01H6FkJUTjDNxPi6WXA9M3n8","session_id":"235c9f17-c672-4220-8162-6bda60e23e6c","uuid":"c47c23aa-8242-4b7a-8c36-02f1dff601bb"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01CY2J81Z9tkV4Z6iGbB2dAg","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→\n     5→export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {\n     6→  const session = await requireAuth();\n     7→  if (!session) {\n     8→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n     9→  }\n    10→\n    11→  try {\n    12→    const body = await req.json();\n    13→    const {\n    14→      bookTitle,\n    15→      bookAuthor,\n    16→      bookCoverUrl,\n    17→      bookOlid,\n    18→      language,\n    19→      readingPace,\n    20→      startDate,\n    21→      meetingFormat,\n    22→      maxGroupSize,\n    23→    } = body;\n    24→\n    25→    if (!bookTitle || !bookAuthor || !readingPace || !startDate || !meetingFormat || !maxGroupSize) {\n    26→      return NextResponse.json(\n    27→        { error: \"Missing required fields\" },\n    28→        { status: 400 }\n    29→      );\n    30→    }\n    31→\n    32→    if (![\"voice\", \"text\", \"mixed\"].includes(meetingFormat)) {\n    33→      return NextResponse.json(\n    34→        { error: \"Invalid meeting format\" },\n    35→        { status: 400 }\n    36→      );\n    37→    }\n    38→\n    39→    const size = parseInt(maxGroupSize, 10);\n    40→    if (isNaN(size) || size < 2 || size > 20) {\n    41→      return NextResponse.json(\n    42→        { error: \"Group size must be between 2 and 20\" },\n    43→        { status: 400 }\n    44→      );\n    45→    }\n    46→\n    47→    const result = db\n    48→      .prepare(\n    49→        `INSERT INTO listings (author_id, book_title, book_author, book_cover_url, book_olid, language, reading_pace, start_date, meeting_format, max_group_size)\n    50→         VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`\n    51→      )\n    52→      .run(\n    53→        session.userId,\n    54→        bookTitle.trim(),\n    55→        bookAuthor.trim(),\n    56→        bookCoverUrl || \"\",\n    57→        bookOlid || \"\",\n    58→        language || \"English\",\n    59→        readingPace.trim(),\n    60→        startDate,\n    61→        meetingFormat,\n    62→        size\n    63→      );\n    64→\n    65→    // Auto-join the author as a member\n    66→    db.prepare(\n    67→      \"INSERT INTO listing_members (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\"\n    68→    ).run(result.lastInsertRowid, session.userId);\n    69→\n    70→    return NextResponse.json(\n    71→      { id: result.lastInsertRowid },\n    72→      { status: 201 }\n    73→    );\n    74→  } catch (error) {\n    75→    console.error(\"Create listing error:\", error);\n    76→    return NextResponse.json(\n    77→      { error: \"Internal server error\" },\n    78→      { status: 500 }\n    79→    );\n    80→  }\n    81→}\n    82→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01H6FkJUTjDNxPi6WXA9M3n8","session_id":"235c9f17-c672-4220-8162-6bda60e23e6c","uuid":"e0a72b12-0157-4dfa-a7df-8ae1d16b97e8","timestamp":"2026-03-19T23:01:29.375Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01H38FwwD7VXJHEmkBWeXTXj","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→\n     4→export async function GET(request: NextRequest) {\n     5→  const { searchParams } = request.nextUrl;\n     6→\n     7→  const q = searchParams.get(\"q\")?.trim() || \"\";\n     8→  const meetingFormat = searchParams.get(\"meeting_format\") || \"\";\n     9→  const readingPace = searchParams.get(\"reading_pace\") || \"\";\n    10→  const sort = searchParams.get(\"sort\") || \"newest\";\n    11→\n    12→  const conditions: string[] = [\"l.is_full = 0\"];\n    13→  const params: (string | number)[] = [];\n    14→\n    15→  if (q) {\n    16→    conditions.push(\"(l.book_title LIKE ? OR l.book_author LIKE ?)\");\n    17→    params.push(`%${q}%`, `%${q}%`);\n    18→  }\n    19→\n    20→  if (meetingFormat && [\"voice\", \"text\", \"mixed\"].includes(meetingFormat)) {\n    21→    conditions.push(\"l.meeting_format = ?\");\n    22→    params.push(meetingFormat);\n    23→  }\n    24→\n    25→  if (readingPace) {\n    26→    conditions.push(\"l.reading_pace LIKE ?\");\n    27→    params.push(`%${readingPace}%`);\n    28→  }\n    29→\n    30→  const whereClause = conditions.join(\" AND \");\n    31→\n    32→  let orderClause = \"l.created_at DESC\";\n    33→  if (sort === \"oldest\") orderClause = \"l.created_at ASC\";\n    34→  else if (sort === \"start_date\") orderClause = \"l.start_date ASC\";\n    35→\n    36→  const listings = db\n    37→    .prepare(\n    38→      `SELECT\n    39→        l.*,\n    40→        u.display_name as author_name,\n    41→        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = l.id) as member_count\n    42→      FROM listings l\n    43→      JOIN users u ON l.author_id = u.id\n    44→      WHERE ${whereClause}\n    45→      ORDER BY ${orderClause}`\n    46→    )\n    47→    .all(...params);\n    48→\n    49→  return NextResponse.json({ listings });\n    50→}\n    51→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01H6FkJUTjDNxPi6WXA9M3n8","session_id":"235c9f17-c672-4220-8162-6bda60e23e6c","uuid":"08e82631-d632-44d5-af13-bf8c9d953a88","timestamp":"2026-03-19T23:01:29.734Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_019oyXKjd3Fn8jbS1Yw3UAKU","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→\n     5→export async function GET() {\n     6→  const session = await requireAuth();\n     7→  if (!session) {\n     8→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n     9→  }\n    10→\n    11→  const notifications = db\n    12→    .prepare(\n    13→      `SELECT n.*, l.book_title\n    14→       FROM notifications n\n    15→       LEFT JOIN listings l ON n.listing_id = l.id\n    16→       WHERE n.user_id = ?\n    17→       ORDER BY n.created_at DESC\n    18→       LIMIT 50`\n    19→    )\n    20→    .all(session.userId);\n    21→\n    22→  const unreadCount = db\n    23→    .prepare(\n    24→      \"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM notifications WHERE user_id = ? AND is_read = 0\"\n    25→    )\n    26→    .get(session.userId) as { count: number };\n    27→\n    28→  return NextResponse.json({\n    29→    notifications,\n    30→    unreadCount: unreadCount.count,\n    31→  });\n    32→}\n    33→\n    34→export async function PATCH(req: NextRequest) {\n    35→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    36→  if (!session) {\n    37→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    38→  }\n    39→\n    40→  const { notificationId } = await req.json();\n    41→\n    42→  if (notificationId) {\n    43→    db.prepare(\n    44→      \"UPDATE notifications SET is_read = 1 WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?\"\n    45→    ).run(notificationId, session.userId);\n    46→  } else {\n    47→    // Mark all as read\n    48→    db.prepare(\n    49→      \"UPDATE notifications SET is_read = 1 WHERE user_id = ?\"\n    50→    ).run(session.userId);\n    51→  }\n    52→\n    53→  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n    54→}\n    55→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01H6FkJUTjDNxPi6WXA9M3n8","session_id":"235c9f17-c672-4220-8162-6bda60e23e6c","uuid":"e77f0acd-3667-4795-92ce-283fcd75f739","timestamp":"2026-03-19T23:01:29.827Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_012vvTgK9orthJkmjYSg5Dsm","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { getIronSession, type SessionOptions } from \"iron-session\";\n     2→import { cookies } from \"next/headers\";\n     3→\n     4→export interface SessionData {\n     5→  userId?: number;\n     6→  email?: string;\n     7→  displayName?: string;\n     8→}\n     9→\n    10→const sessionOptions: SessionOptions = {\n    11→  password:\n    12→    process.env.SESSION_SECRET ||\n    13→    \"bookmate-development-secret-key-change-in-production-32chars!\",\n    14→  cookieName: \"bookmate_session\",\n    15→  cookieOptions: {\n    16→    httpOnly: true,\n    17→    secure: process.env.NODE_ENV === \"production\",\n    18→    sameSite: \"lax\",\n    19→    maxAge: 60 * 60 * 24 * 7, // 1 week\n    20→  },\n    21→};\n    22→\n    23→export async function getSession() {\n    24→  const cookieStore = await cookies();\n    25→  return getIronSession<SessionData>(cookieStore, sessionOptions);\n    26→}\n    27→\n    28→export async function requireAuth() {\n    29→  const session = await getSession();\n    30→  if (!session.userId) {\n    31→    return null;\n    32→  }\n    33→  return session;\n    34→}\n    35→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01H6FkJUTjDNxPi6WXA9M3n8","session_id":"235c9f17-c672-4220-8162-6bda60e23e6c","uuid":"16356461-0040-49e2-a85f-2d825ced5134","timestamp":"2026-03-19T23:01:35.112Z"}
