{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01BfnZdsXHjgonEQGk5rfVs4","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_012nnMGAL4sQfRCRVnTQ9ZqY","name":"Read","input":{"file_path":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/telegram/route.ts"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":4175,"cache_read_input_tokens":19094,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":4175,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01H6FkJUTjDNxPi6WXA9M3n8","session_id":"235c9f17-c672-4220-8162-6bda60e23e6c","uuid":"84ca8c81-a7ef-40ac-8629-92b2d0cdf791"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_012nnMGAL4sQfRCRVnTQ9ZqY","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→\n     5→export async function POST(\n     6→  req: NextRequest,\n     7→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n     8→) {\n     9→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    10→  if (!session) {\n    11→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    12→  }\n    13→\n    14→  const { id } = await params;\n    15→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    16→\n    17→  const listing = db\n    18→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    19→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    20→\n    21→  if (!listing) {\n    22→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    23→  }\n    24→\n    25→  if (listing.author_id !== session.userId) {\n    26→    return NextResponse.json(\n    27→      { error: \"Only the listing author can set the Telegram link\" },\n    28→      { status: 403 }\n    29→    );\n    30→  }\n    31→\n    32→  const { telegramLink } = await req.json();\n    33→\n    34→  if (!telegramLink || !telegramLink.startsWith(\"https://t.me/\")) {\n    35→    return NextResponse.json(\n    36→      { error: \"Please provide a valid Telegram invite link (https://t.me/...)\" },\n    37→      { status: 400 }\n    38→    );\n    39→  }\n    40→\n    41→  db.prepare(\"UPDATE listings SET telegram_link = ? WHERE id = ?\").run(\n    42→    telegramLink.trim(),\n    43→    listingId\n    44→  );\n    45→\n    46→  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n    47→}\n    48→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01H6FkJUTjDNxPi6WXA9M3n8","session_id":"235c9f17-c672-4220-8162-6bda60e23e6c","uuid":"b865afc7-af32-409c-b14d-2ee0fb9996dc","timestamp":"2026-03-19T23:01:35.396Z"}
{"type":"system","subtype":"task_notification","task_id":"abf4692fecf04c5cd","tool_use_id":"toolu_01H6FkJUTjDNxPi6WXA9M3n8","status":"completed","output_file":"","summary":"Design approval system architecture","usage":{"total_tokens":24539,"tool_uses":13,"duration_ms":103633},"uuid":"08ea9cfe-b4c3-4995-95fe-a3e2d238cf95","session_id":"235c9f17-c672-4220-8162-6bda60e23e6c"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01H6FkJUTjDNxPi6WXA9M3n8","type":"tool_result","content":[{"type":"text","text":"I now have a complete picture of the codebase. Here is the full architecture blueprint.\n\n---\n\n## Patterns & Conventions Found\n\n**DB schema** (`/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/db.ts`): Schema is defined inline in `db.exec()` using `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS`. Migrations are additive `ALTER TABLE` statements appended after the `exec` block. Foreign keys are ON, WAL mode is set. The `is_full` column is an INTEGER 0/1 boolean — this is the established SQLite boolean pattern.\n\n**API routes** (`app/app/api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts`): Each sub-action lives in its own `route.ts` under `[id]/<action>/`. Auth is always the first check via `requireAuth()`. Validation errors return `{ error: \"...\" }` with 4xx. All DB calls are synchronous better-sqlite3 (no async/await for DB). The author auto-joins as a member on listing creation.\n\n**Notifications** (`/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/notifications.ts`): A pure utility module. Notification `type` strings are freeform (e.g. `\"new_member\"`, `\"group_full\"`). All notification fan-out is done from within the API route, not via triggers. The `createNotification` function is the single insertion primitive.\n\n**Listing detail page** (`app/app/listings/[id]/page.tsx`): One `fetchListing()` function fetches all state and is re-called after mutations. The UI branches on `listing.isMember`, `listing.isAuthor`, and `listing.is_full`. The API GET endpoint at `/api/listings/[id]` computes and returns `isMember` and `isAuthor` as derived booleans.\n\n**Member count / fullness**: The `is_full` flag on `listings` is set at join time when `count + 1 >= max_group_size`. The listing index route (`/api/listings`) filters `WHERE l.is_full = 0`.\n\n---\n\n## Architecture Decision\n\nThe design centers on a new `listing_applications` join table. The `listings` table gets one new column: `requires_approval INTEGER DEFAULT 0`. The approval workflow replaces the join route's behavior when `requires_approval = 1` — the route inserts into `listing_applications` instead of `listing_members`.\n\nA new `[id]/apply/route.ts` handles the application action, and a new `[id]/applications/route.ts` (PATCH only) handles approve/reject decisions by the author. The listing GET endpoint is extended to return pending applicants when the viewer is the author. The listing detail page UI branches on the new `requires_approval`, `hasApplied`, and `pendingApplicants` fields.\n\nThe author is not subject to the approval flow — they auto-join as a member on creation, exactly as today. The `is_full` threshold is calculated from approved members only (the `listing_members` count), not from applicants.\n\n**Trade-off accepted**: `listing_applications` is a separate table rather than adding a `status` column to `listing_members`. This keeps the `listing_members` table semantically clean (only approved/direct members), avoids touching the existing join queries in the listing index and telegram routes, and makes the approval panel query straightforward.\n\n---\n\n## DB Schema Changes\n\n### New column on `listings`\n\n```sql\nALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN requires_approval INTEGER DEFAULT 0;\n```\n\nAdded via `db.exec()` at the bottom of the schema init block in `db.ts`, wrapped in a `try/catch` since SQLite `ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN` errors if the column already exists (for idempotent restarts).\n\n### New table: `listing_applications`\n\n```sql\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listing_applications (\n  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n  listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n  user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n  status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending'\n    CHECK(status IN ('pending', 'approved', 'rejected')),\n  message TEXT DEFAULT '',\n  applied_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n  decided_at TEXT,\n  UNIQUE(listing_id, user_id),\n  FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id),\n  FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n);\n```\n\nThe `UNIQUE(listing_id, user_id)` constraint prevents double-applying. The `status` CHECK constraint enforces the three valid states at the DB level, consistent with the `meeting_format` CHECK pattern on `listings`.\n\n---\n\n## Component Design\n\n### Files to CREATE\n\n**`/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/apply/route.ts`**\n\nResponsibility: Handle a user's application to join an approval-gated listing.\n\n- `POST`: Requires auth. Validates listing exists, is not full, `requires_approval = 1`, user is not already a member, user has not already applied. Inserts into `listing_applications` with `status = 'pending'`. Calls `notifyApplicationReceived(listingId, applicantName)` (new notification helper). Returns `{ ok: true }`.\n\n**`/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/applications/route.ts`**\n\nResponsibility: Author-only endpoint to approve or reject a pending application.\n\n- `PATCH`: Requires auth, requires `session.userId === listing.author_id`. Body: `{ applicationId: number, action: 'approve' | 'reject' }`. Runs inside a `db.transaction()`:\n  - Updates `listing_applications SET status = ?, decided_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ? AND listing_id = ?`\n  - On `'approve'`: Inserts into `listing_members`. Calls `notifyApplicationDecision(userId, listingId, 'approved')`. Checks member count against `max_group_size` and sets `is_full = 1` + calls `notifyGroupFull` if reached.\n  - On `'reject'`: Calls `notifyApplicationDecision(userId, listingId, 'rejected')` only.\n  - Returns `{ ok: true }`.\n\nThe transaction ensures the member insert and status update are atomic — no applicant can be double-approved.\n\n### Files to MODIFY\n\n**`/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/db.ts`**\n\nAdd the new table and the `ALTER TABLE` migration after the existing `db.exec()` block:\n\n```ts\ndb.exec(`\n  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listing_applications (\n    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending'\n      CHECK(status IN ('pending', 'approved', 'rejected')),\n    message TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    applied_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    decided_at TEXT,\n    UNIQUE(listing_id, user_id),\n    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id),\n    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n  );\n`);\n\n// Idempotent migration: add requires_approval if it doesn't exist yet\ntry {\n  db.exec(\"ALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN requires_approval INTEGER DEFAULT 0\");\n} catch {\n  // Column already exists — safe to ignore\n}\n```\n\n**`/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/notifications.ts`**\n\nAdd two new exported functions:\n\n- `notifyApplicationReceived(listingId, applicantName)`: Notifies the listing author with type `\"application_received\"` — message: `\"${applicantName} applied to join your reading group for \\\"${book_title}\\\"\"`.\n- `notifyApplicationDecision(userId, listingId, decision: 'approved' | 'rejected')`: Notifies the applicant with type `\"application_approved\"` or `\"application_rejected\"`. Approved message: `\"Your application to join \\\"${book_title}\\\" was approved!\"`. Rejected message: `\"Your application to join \\\"${book_title}\\\" was not accepted.\"`.\n\n**`/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts`**\n\nAt the top of the POST handler, after fetching the listing, add a guard:\n\n```ts\nif (listing.requires_approval) {\n  return NextResponse.json(\n    { error: \"This group requires approval. Use the apply endpoint.\" },\n    { status: 400 }\n  );\n}\n```\n\nThis prevents the direct-join path from bypassing the approval gate on old clients or manual API calls.\n\n**`/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts`**\n\nThe GET handler currently returns `isMember` and `isAuthor`. Extend it to also return:\n\n1. `requiresApproval: boolean` — directly from `listing.requires_approval`.\n2. `hasApplied: boolean` — query `listing_applications WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ? AND status = 'pending'`. Only meaningful when `session.userId` exists and `!isMember`.\n3. `pendingApplicants: Applicant[]` — only populated when `isAuthor`. Query:\n   ```sql\n   SELECT la.id as application_id, u.id, u.display_name, u.bio,\n          la.message, la.applied_at\n   FROM listing_applications la\n   JOIN users u ON la.user_id = u.id\n   WHERE la.listing_id = ? AND la.status = 'pending'\n   ORDER BY la.applied_at ASC\n   ```\n\n**`/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/create/route.ts`**\n\nAccept `requiresApproval` (boolean/0/1) from the request body. Pass it to the INSERT:\n\n```sql\nINSERT INTO listings (..., requires_approval) VALUES (..., ?)\n```\n\nValidate that it is a boolean or 0/1 integer. The author still auto-joins as a member regardless of this flag.\n\n**`/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/listings/create/page.tsx`**\n\nAdd a `requiresApproval` boolean state (default `false`). Add a toggle control in the form between the \"Maximum group size\" field and the submit button:\n\n```\n[ ] Require approval for new members\n    Members must be approved by you before joining.\n```\n\nUse the existing pill-button / border pattern for the toggle or a styled checkbox. Include `requiresApproval` in the `handleSubmit` JSON body.\n\n**`/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/listings/[id]/page.tsx`**\n\nExtend the `Listing` interface to include:\n\n```ts\nrequires_approval: number;\nhasApplied: boolean;\npendingApplicants: Applicant[];  // new interface: application_id, id, display_name, bio, message, applied_at\n```\n\n**Join/Apply button area** — replace the single `!listing.isMember && !listing.is_full` block with three branches:\n\n1. `!listing.isMember && !listing.is_full && !listing.requires_approval` → existing \"Join this group\" button calling `handleJoin`.\n2. `!listing.isMember && !listing.is_full && listing.requires_approval && !listing.hasApplied` → \"Apply to join\" button calling `handleApply` (new handler that POSTs to `/api/listings/${id}/apply`). After success, re-call `fetchListing()`.\n3. `!listing.isMember && listing.hasApplied` → a static badge: \"Application pending\" (styled like the existing \"You're a member\" badge, in amber/accent color).\n\n**Applicant management panel** — add a new card section above the Members card, visible only when `listing.isAuthor && listing.requires_approval && listing.pendingApplicants.length > 0`:\n\n```\nPending Applicants (N)\n[avatar] Name — bio snippet      [Approve] [Reject]\n[avatar] Name — bio snippet      [Approve] [Reject]\n```\n\nEach row calls `handleDecision(applicationId, 'approve' | 'reject')` — a new handler that PATCHes `/api/listings/${id}/applications` with `{ applicationId, action }`, then re-calls `fetchListing()`. The approve/reject buttons use `btn-primary` and a secondary destructive style respectively, consistent with the existing button patterns.\n\n---\n\n## Data Flow\n\n```\nCREATE LISTING\n  Client POSTs requiresApproval=true\n  → /api/listings/create\n  → INSERT listings (requires_approval=1)\n  → INSERT listing_members (author auto-joins)\n  → returns { id }\n\nUSER APPLIES\n  Client POSTs to /api/listings/:id/apply\n  → validate: not member, not full, not already applied\n  → INSERT listing_applications (status='pending')\n  → notifyApplicationReceived → author gets notification\n  → returns { ok: true }\n  Client: listing.hasApplied = true → \"Application pending\" badge\n\nAUTHOR APPROVES\n  Client PATCHes /api/listings/:id/applications { applicationId, action: 'approve' }\n  → transaction:\n      UPDATE listing_applications SET status='approved'\n      INSERT listing_members\n      count members → if >= max_group_size: UPDATE listings SET is_full=1\n  → notifyApplicationDecision(userId, 'approved') → applicant notified\n  → if full: notifyGroupFull → all members notified\n  → returns { ok: true }\n  Client: re-fetches → applicant disappears from panel, appears in members list\n\nAUTHOR REJECTS\n  Client PATCHes /api/listings/:id/applications { applicationId, action: 'reject' }\n  → UPDATE listing_applications SET status='rejected'\n  → notifyApplicationDecision(userId, 'rejected') → applicant notified\n  → returns { ok: true }\n\nLISTING INDEX (/api/listings GET)\n  No change needed — filters by is_full=0, which is still driven by listing_members count\n```\n\n---\n\n## Implementation Sequence\n\n**Phase 1 — DB & Server Foundation**\n\n- [ ] Modify `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/db.ts`: Add `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listing_applications` and the `try/catch ALTER TABLE` migration for `requires_approval`.\n- [ ] Modify `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/notifications.ts`: Add `notifyApplicationReceived` and `notifyApplicationDecision`.\n- [ ] Modify `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/create/route.ts`: Accept and persist `requiresApproval`.\n- [ ] Modify `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts`: Add the approval-gate guard.\n\n**Phase 2 — New API Endpoints**\n\n- [ ] Create `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/apply/route.ts`.\n- [ ] Create `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/applications/route.ts`.\n\n**Phase 3 — Extend the GET Endpoint**\n\n- [ ] Modify `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts`: Add `requiresApproval`, `hasApplied`, and `pendingApplicants` to the response.\n\n**Phase 4 — UI**\n\n- [ ] Modify `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/listings/create/page.tsx`: Add `requiresApproval` toggle and include it in the submit payload.\n- [ ] Modify `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/listings/[id]/page.tsx`: Extend the `Listing` interface, add the apply/pending states to the action area, and add the applicant management panel.\n\n---\n\n## Critical Details\n\n**Atomicity**: The approve action MUST use `db.transaction()`. Without it, a race condition exists where two concurrent approvals of two different applicants could both succeed even if only one spot remains (both check count before either inserts). The transaction serializes this because better-sqlite3 transactions are synchronous and exclusive.\n\n**Idempotency of the migration**: The `ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN` is wrapped in try/catch because SQLite throws `table listings already has a column named requires_approval` on subsequent server restarts. This is the correct SQLite migration pattern — there is no `IF NOT EXISTS` for `ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN` in SQLite.\n\n**Backward compatibility**: Existing listings all have `requires_approval = 0` by default (the column default). All existing join flows remain untouched for those listings. No existing data is modified.\n\n**Re-application after rejection**: The `UNIQUE(listing_id, user_id)` constraint on `listing_applications` means a rejected user cannot re-apply without a DB-level change. If re-application is desired, the apply route should `UPDATE listing_applications SET status='pending', decided_at=NULL WHERE listing_id=? AND user_id=? AND status='rejected'` as an upsert, instead of a plain INSERT. This is a product decision — implement the upsert if re-application should be allowed.\n\n**Listing index display**: The listing index does not need to show applicant counts. However, it may be worth adding a visual indicator on listing cards that the group `requires_approval` so users know before clicking. This is a low-priority enhancement — the card component (not audited here) would need a badge.\n\n**Security**: The `applications` PATCH endpoint must validate that `applicationId` belongs to the `listingId` in the URL, not just that the author owns the listing. The query should be `WHERE id = ? AND listing_id = ?` to prevent an author from approving applications on listings they don't own by guessing IDs.\n\n**Notification types for the frontend**: The notifications page (`/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/notifications/`) should already render the new notification types correctly since it displays the raw `message` string. No changes needed there unless you want type-specific icons."},{"type":"text","text":"agentId: abf4692fecf04c5cd (use SendMessage with to: 'abf4692fecf04c5cd' to continue this agent)\n<usage>total_tokens: 28740\ntool_uses: 13\nduration_ms: 103636</usage>"}]}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"235c9f17-c672-4220-8162-6bda60e23e6c","uuid":"2278e764-dc29-4864-b503-25fa5b9123fc","timestamp":"2026-03-19T23:03:00.519Z","tool_use_result":{"status":"completed","prompt":"I need to implement an Applicant Approval System for the Bookmate app (Next.js 15, App Router, SQLite via better-sqlite3, iron-session auth).\n\nCurrent behavior: When users join a listing, it's first-come-first-served (auto-join). Listings auto-close when member count reaches max_group_size.\n\nNew behavior: Listing authors can optionally enable an approval system when creating a listing. When enabled:\n- Users \"apply\" instead of directly joining\n- The listing author sees pending applicants and can approve/reject each\n- Approved users become members\n- Rejected users are notified\n- The listing closes when approved members reach max_group_size\n\nPlease analyze the existing codebase and design the implementation:\n1. Read the DB schema in app/lib/db.ts\n2. Read the listing creation page at app/app/listings/create/page.tsx\n3. Read the listing detail page at app/app/listings/[id]/page.tsx\n4. Read the listing API routes at app/app/api/listings/\n5. Read the notifications system at app/lib/notifications.ts and app/app/api/notifications/\n\nProvide:\n- DB schema changes needed (new columns, new tables)\n- API endpoint changes/additions\n- UI changes needed\n- Implementation sequence","agentId":"abf4692fecf04c5cd","content":[{"type":"text","text":"I now have a complete picture of the codebase. Here is the full architecture blueprint.\n\n---\n\n## Patterns & Conventions Found\n\n**DB schema** (`/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/db.ts`): Schema is defined inline in `db.exec()` using `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS`. Migrations are additive `ALTER TABLE` statements appended after the `exec` block. Foreign keys are ON, WAL mode is set. The `is_full` column is an INTEGER 0/1 boolean — this is the established SQLite boolean pattern.\n\n**API routes** (`app/app/api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts`): Each sub-action lives in its own `route.ts` under `[id]/<action>/`. Auth is always the first check via `requireAuth()`. Validation errors return `{ error: \"...\" }` with 4xx. All DB calls are synchronous better-sqlite3 (no async/await for DB). The author auto-joins as a member on listing creation.\n\n**Notifications** (`/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/notifications.ts`): A pure utility module. Notification `type` strings are freeform (e.g. `\"new_member\"`, `\"group_full\"`). All notification fan-out is done from within the API route, not via triggers. The `createNotification` function is the single insertion primitive.\n\n**Listing detail page** (`app/app/listings/[id]/page.tsx`): One `fetchListing()` function fetches all state and is re-called after mutations. The UI branches on `listing.isMember`, `listing.isAuthor`, and `listing.is_full`. The API GET endpoint at `/api/listings/[id]` computes and returns `isMember` and `isAuthor` as derived booleans.\n\n**Member count / fullness**: The `is_full` flag on `listings` is set at join time when `count + 1 >= max_group_size`. The listing index route (`/api/listings`) filters `WHERE l.is_full = 0`.\n\n---\n\n## Architecture Decision\n\nThe design centers on a new `listing_applications` join table. The `listings` table gets one new column: `requires_approval INTEGER DEFAULT 0`. The approval workflow replaces the join route's behavior when `requires_approval = 1` — the route inserts into `listing_applications` instead of `listing_members`.\n\nA new `[id]/apply/route.ts` handles the application action, and a new `[id]/applications/route.ts` (PATCH only) handles approve/reject decisions by the author. The listing GET endpoint is extended to return pending applicants when the viewer is the author. The listing detail page UI branches on the new `requires_approval`, `hasApplied`, and `pendingApplicants` fields.\n\nThe author is not subject to the approval flow — they auto-join as a member on creation, exactly as today. The `is_full` threshold is calculated from approved members only (the `listing_members` count), not from applicants.\n\n**Trade-off accepted**: `listing_applications` is a separate table rather than adding a `status` column to `listing_members`. This keeps the `listing_members` table semantically clean (only approved/direct members), avoids touching the existing join queries in the listing index and telegram routes, and makes the approval panel query straightforward.\n\n---\n\n## DB Schema Changes\n\n### New column on `listings`\n\n```sql\nALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN requires_approval INTEGER DEFAULT 0;\n```\n\nAdded via `db.exec()` at the bottom of the schema init block in `db.ts`, wrapped in a `try/catch` since SQLite `ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN` errors if the column already exists (for idempotent restarts).\n\n### New table: `listing_applications`\n\n```sql\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listing_applications (\n  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n  listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n  user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n  status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending'\n    CHECK(status IN ('pending', 'approved', 'rejected')),\n  message TEXT DEFAULT '',\n  applied_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n  decided_at TEXT,\n  UNIQUE(listing_id, user_id),\n  FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id),\n  FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n);\n```\n\nThe `UNIQUE(listing_id, user_id)` constraint prevents double-applying. The `status` CHECK constraint enforces the three valid states at the DB level, consistent with the `meeting_format` CHECK pattern on `listings`.\n\n---\n\n## Component Design\n\n### Files to CREATE\n\n**`/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/apply/route.ts`**\n\nResponsibility: Handle a user's application to join an approval-gated listing.\n\n- `POST`: Requires auth. Validates listing exists, is not full, `requires_approval = 1`, user is not already a member, user has not already applied. Inserts into `listing_applications` with `status = 'pending'`. Calls `notifyApplicationReceived(listingId, applicantName)` (new notification helper). Returns `{ ok: true }`.\n\n**`/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/applications/route.ts`**\n\nResponsibility: Author-only endpoint to approve or reject a pending application.\n\n- `PATCH`: Requires auth, requires `session.userId === listing.author_id`. Body: `{ applicationId: number, action: 'approve' | 'reject' }`. Runs inside a `db.transaction()`:\n  - Updates `listing_applications SET status = ?, decided_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ? AND listing_id = ?`\n  - On `'approve'`: Inserts into `listing_members`. Calls `notifyApplicationDecision(userId, listingId, 'approved')`. Checks member count against `max_group_size` and sets `is_full = 1` + calls `notifyGroupFull` if reached.\n  - On `'reject'`: Calls `notifyApplicationDecision(userId, listingId, 'rejected')` only.\n  - Returns `{ ok: true }`.\n\nThe transaction ensures the member insert and status update are atomic — no applicant can be double-approved.\n\n### Files to MODIFY\n\n**`/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/db.ts`**\n\nAdd the new table and the `ALTER TABLE` migration after the existing `db.exec()` block:\n\n```ts\ndb.exec(`\n  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listing_applications (\n    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending'\n      CHECK(status IN ('pending', 'approved', 'rejected')),\n    message TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    applied_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    decided_at TEXT,\n    UNIQUE(listing_id, user_id),\n    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id),\n    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n  );\n`);\n\n// Idempotent migration: add requires_approval if it doesn't exist yet\ntry {\n  db.exec(\"ALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN requires_approval INTEGER DEFAULT 0\");\n} catch {\n  // Column already exists — safe to ignore\n}\n```\n\n**`/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/notifications.ts`**\n\nAdd two new exported functions:\n\n- `notifyApplicationReceived(listingId, applicantName)`: Notifies the listing author with type `\"application_received\"` — message: `\"${applicantName} applied to join your reading group for \\\"${book_title}\\\"\"`.\n- `notifyApplicationDecision(userId, listingId, decision: 'approved' | 'rejected')`: Notifies the applicant with type `\"application_approved\"` or `\"application_rejected\"`. Approved message: `\"Your application to join \\\"${book_title}\\\" was approved!\"`. Rejected message: `\"Your application to join \\\"${book_title}\\\" was not accepted.\"`.\n\n**`/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts`**\n\nAt the top of the POST handler, after fetching the listing, add a guard:\n\n```ts\nif (listing.requires_approval) {\n  return NextResponse.json(\n    { error: \"This group requires approval. Use the apply endpoint.\" },\n    { status: 400 }\n  );\n}\n```\n\nThis prevents the direct-join path from bypassing the approval gate on old clients or manual API calls.\n\n**`/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts`**\n\nThe GET handler currently returns `isMember` and `isAuthor`. Extend it to also return:\n\n1. `requiresApproval: boolean` — directly from `listing.requires_approval`.\n2. `hasApplied: boolean` — query `listing_applications WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ? AND status = 'pending'`. Only meaningful when `session.userId` exists and `!isMember`.\n3. `pendingApplicants: Applicant[]` — only populated when `isAuthor`. Query:\n   ```sql\n   SELECT la.id as application_id, u.id, u.display_name, u.bio,\n          la.message, la.applied_at\n   FROM listing_applications la\n   JOIN users u ON la.user_id = u.id\n   WHERE la.listing_id = ? AND la.status = 'pending'\n   ORDER BY la.applied_at ASC\n   ```\n\n**`/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/create/route.ts`**\n\nAccept `requiresApproval` (boolean/0/1) from the request body. Pass it to the INSERT:\n\n```sql\nINSERT INTO listings (..., requires_approval) VALUES (..., ?)\n```\n\nValidate that it is a boolean or 0/1 integer. The author still auto-joins as a member regardless of this flag.\n\n**`/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/listings/create/page.tsx`**\n\nAdd a `requiresApproval` boolean state (default `false`). Add a toggle control in the form between the \"Maximum group size\" field and the submit button:\n\n```\n[ ] Require approval for new members\n    Members must be approved by you before joining.\n```\n\nUse the existing pill-button / border pattern for the toggle or a styled checkbox. Include `requiresApproval` in the `handleSubmit` JSON body.\n\n**`/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/listings/[id]/page.tsx`**\n\nExtend the `Listing` interface to include:\n\n```ts\nrequires_approval: number;\nhasApplied: boolean;\npendingApplicants: Applicant[];  // new interface: application_id, id, display_name, bio, message, applied_at\n```\n\n**Join/Apply button area** — replace the single `!listing.isMember && !listing.is_full` block with three branches:\n\n1. `!listing.isMember && !listing.is_full && !listing.requires_approval` → existing \"Join this group\" button calling `handleJoin`.\n2. `!listing.isMember && !listing.is_full && listing.requires_approval && !listing.hasApplied` → \"Apply to join\" button calling `handleApply` (new handler that POSTs to `/api/listings/${id}/apply`). After success, re-call `fetchListing()`.\n3. `!listing.isMember && listing.hasApplied` → a static badge: \"Application pending\" (styled like the existing \"You're a member\" badge, in amber/accent color).\n\n**Applicant management panel** — add a new card section above the Members card, visible only when `listing.isAuthor && listing.requires_approval && listing.pendingApplicants.length > 0`:\n\n```\nPending Applicants (N)\n[avatar] Name — bio snippet      [Approve] [Reject]\n[avatar] Name — bio snippet      [Approve] [Reject]\n```\n\nEach row calls `handleDecision(applicationId, 'approve' | 'reject')` — a new handler that PATCHes `/api/listings/${id}/applications` with `{ applicationId, action }`, then re-calls `fetchListing()`. The approve/reject buttons use `btn-primary` and a secondary destructive style respectively, consistent with the existing button patterns.\n\n---\n\n## Data Flow\n\n```\nCREATE LISTING\n  Client POSTs requiresApproval=true\n  → /api/listings/create\n  → INSERT listings (requires_approval=1)\n  → INSERT listing_members (author auto-joins)\n  → returns { id }\n\nUSER APPLIES\n  Client POSTs to /api/listings/:id/apply\n  → validate: not member, not full, not already applied\n  → INSERT listing_applications (status='pending')\n  → notifyApplicationReceived → author gets notification\n  → returns { ok: true }\n  Client: listing.hasApplied = true → \"Application pending\" badge\n\nAUTHOR APPROVES\n  Client PATCHes /api/listings/:id/applications { applicationId, action: 'approve' }\n  → transaction:\n      UPDATE listing_applications SET status='approved'\n      INSERT listing_members\n      count members → if >= max_group_size: UPDATE listings SET is_full=1\n  → notifyApplicationDecision(userId, 'approved') → applicant notified\n  → if full: notifyGroupFull → all members notified\n  → returns { ok: true }\n  Client: re-fetches → applicant disappears from panel, appears in members list\n\nAUTHOR REJECTS\n  Client PATCHes /api/listings/:id/applications { applicationId, action: 'reject' }\n  → UPDATE listing_applications SET status='rejected'\n  → notifyApplicationDecision(userId, 'rejected') → applicant notified\n  → returns { ok: true }\n\nLISTING INDEX (/api/listings GET)\n  No change needed — filters by is_full=0, which is still driven by listing_members count\n```\n\n---\n\n## Implementation Sequence\n\n**Phase 1 — DB & Server Foundation**\n\n- [ ] Modify `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/db.ts`: Add `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listing_applications` and the `try/catch ALTER TABLE` migration for `requires_approval`.\n- [ ] Modify `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/notifications.ts`: Add `notifyApplicationReceived` and `notifyApplicationDecision`.\n- [ ] Modify `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/create/route.ts`: Accept and persist `requiresApproval`.\n- [ ] Modify `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts`: Add the approval-gate guard.\n\n**Phase 2 — New API Endpoints**\n\n- [ ] Create `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/apply/route.ts`.\n- [ ] Create `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/applications/route.ts`.\n\n**Phase 3 — Extend the GET Endpoint**\n\n- [ ] Modify `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts`: Add `requiresApproval`, `hasApplied`, and `pendingApplicants` to the response.\n\n**Phase 4 — UI**\n\n- [ ] Modify `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/listings/create/page.tsx`: Add `requiresApproval` toggle and include it in the submit payload.\n- [ ] Modify `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/listings/[id]/page.tsx`: Extend the `Listing` interface, add the apply/pending states to the action area, and add the applicant management panel.\n\n---\n\n## Critical Details\n\n**Atomicity**: The approve action MUST use `db.transaction()`. Without it, a race condition exists where two concurrent approvals of two different applicants could both succeed even if only one spot remains (both check count before either inserts). The transaction serializes this because better-sqlite3 transactions are synchronous and exclusive.\n\n**Idempotency of the migration**: The `ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN` is wrapped in try/catch because SQLite throws `table listings already has a column named requires_approval` on subsequent server restarts. This is the correct SQLite migration pattern — there is no `IF NOT EXISTS` for `ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN` in SQLite.\n\n**Backward compatibility**: Existing listings all have `requires_approval = 0` by default (the column default). All existing join flows remain untouched for those listings. No existing data is modified.\n\n**Re-application after rejection**: The `UNIQUE(listing_id, user_id)` constraint on `listing_applications` means a rejected user cannot re-apply without a DB-level change. If re-application is desired, the apply route should `UPDATE listing_applications SET status='pending', decided_at=NULL WHERE listing_id=? AND user_id=? AND status='rejected'` as an upsert, instead of a plain INSERT. This is a product decision — implement the upsert if re-application should be allowed.\n\n**Listing index display**: The listing index does not need to show applicant counts. However, it may be worth adding a visual indicator on listing cards that the group `requires_approval` so users know before clicking. This is a low-priority enhancement — the card component (not audited here) would need a badge.\n\n**Security**: The `applications` PATCH endpoint must validate that `applicationId` belongs to the `listingId` in the URL, not just that the author owns the listing. The query should be `WHERE id = ? AND listing_id = ?` to prevent an author from approving applications on listings they don't own by guessing IDs.\n\n**Notification types for the frontend**: The notifications page (`/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/notifications/`) should already render the new notification types correctly since it displays the raw `message` string. No changes needed there unless you want type-specific icons."}],"totalDurationMs":103636,"totalTokens":28740,"totalToolUseCount":13,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1127,"cache_read_input_tokens":23269,"output_tokens":4343,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":1127},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"}}}

 7→\n     8→// Ensure data directory exists\n     9→if (!fs.existsSync(DB_DIR)) {\n    10→  fs.mkdirSync(DB_DIR, { recursive: true });\n    11→}\n    12→\n    13→const db = new Database(DB_PATH);\n    14→\n    15→// Enable WAL mode for better concurrent performance\n    16→db.pragma(\"journal_mode = WAL\");\n    17→db.pragma(\"foreign_keys = ON\");\n    18→\n    19→// Initialize schema\n    20→db.exec(`\n    21→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (\n    22→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    23→    email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,\n    24→    password_hash TEXT NOT NULL,\n    25→    display_name TEXT NOT NULL,\n    26→    bio TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    27→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))\n    28→  );\n    29→\n    30→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listings (\n    31→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    32→    author_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    33→    book_title TEXT NOT NULL,\n    34→    book_author TEXT NOT NULL,\n    35→    book_cover_url TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    36→    book_olid TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    37→    language TEXT DEFAULT 'English',\n    38→    reading_pace TEXT NOT NULL,\n    39→    start_date TEXT NOT NULL,\n    40→    meeting_format TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(meeting_format IN ('voice', 'text', 'mixed')),\n    41→    max_group_size INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK(max_group_size >= 2 AND max_group_size <= 20),\n    42→    telegram_link TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    43→    is_full INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n    44→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    45→    FOREIGN KEY (author_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    46→  );\n    47→\n    48→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listing_members (\n    49→    listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    50→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    51→    joined_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    52→    PRIMARY KEY (listing_id, user_id),\n    53→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id),\n    54→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    55→  );\n    56→\n    57→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS user_genres (\n    58→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    59→    genre TEXT NOT NULL,\n    60→    PRIMARY KEY (user_id, genre),\n    61→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    62→  );\n    63→\n    64→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS notifications (\n    65→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    66→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    67→    listing_id INTEGER,\n    68→    type TEXT NOT NULL,\n    69→    message TEXT NOT NULL,\n    70→    is_read INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n    71→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    72→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id),\n    73→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id)\n    74→  );\n    75→`);\n    76→\n    77→export default db;\n    78→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"235c9f17-c672-4220-8162-6bda60e23e6c","uuid":"dd74dbe6-1650-4c26-8c29-7ae856d504b9","timestamp":"2026-03-19T23:03:05.686Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/db.ts","content":"import Database from \"better-sqlite3\";\nimport path from \"path\";\nimport fs from \"fs\";\n\nconst DB_DIR = process.env.DB_DIR || path.join(process.cwd(), \"data\");\nconst DB_PATH = path.join(DB_DIR, \"bookmate.db\");\n\n// Ensure data directory exists\nif (!fs.existsSync(DB_DIR)) {\n  fs.mkdirSync(DB_DIR, { recursive: true });\n}\n\nconst db = new Database(DB_PATH);\n\n// Enable WAL mode for better concurrent performance\ndb.pragma(\"journal_mode = WAL\");\ndb.pragma(\"foreign_keys = ON\");\n\n// Initialize schema\ndb.exec(`\n  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (\n    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,\n    password_hash TEXT NOT NULL,\n    display_name TEXT NOT NULL,\n    bio TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))\n  );\n\n  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listings (\n    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    author_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    book_title TEXT NOT NULL,\n    book_author TEXT NOT NULL,\n    book_cover_url TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    book_olid TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    language TEXT DEFAULT 'English',\n    reading_pace TEXT NOT NULL,\n    start_date TEXT NOT NULL,\n    meeting_format TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(meeting_format IN ('voice', 'text', 'mixed')),\n    max_group_size INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK(max_group_size >= 2 AND max_group_size <= 20),\n    telegram_link TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    is_full INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    FOREIGN KEY (author_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n  );\n\n  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listing_members (\n    listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    joined_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    PRIMARY KEY (listing_id, user_id),\n    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id),\n    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n  );\n\n  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS user_genres (\n    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    genre TEXT NOT NULL,\n    PRIMARY KEY (user_id, genre),\n    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n  );\n\n  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS notifications (\n    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    listing_id INTEGER,\n    type TEXT NOT NULL,\n    message TEXT NOT NULL,\n    is_read INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id),\n    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id)\n  );\n`);\n\nexport default db;\n","numLines":78,"startLine":1,"totalLines":78}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_018ihB6L8x5K3X3AXCWfUAWo","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import db from \"./db\";\n     2→\n     3→export function createNotification(\n     4→  userId: number,\n     5→  listingId: number,\n     6→  type: string,\n     7→  message: string\n     8→) {\n     9→  db.prepare(\n    10→    \"INSERT INTO notifications (user_id, listing_id, type, message) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)\"\n    11→  ).run(userId, listingId, type, message);\n    12→}\n    13→\n    14→export function notifyListingAuthor(\n    15→  listingId: number,\n    16→  joinerName: string\n    17→) {\n    18→  const listing = db\n    19→    .prepare(\n    20→      \"SELECT l.author_id, l.book_title FROM listings l WHERE l.id = ?\"\n    21→    )\n    22→    .get(listingId) as { author_id: number; book_title: string } | undefined;\n    23→\n    24→  if (listing) {\n    25→    createNotification(\n    26→      listing.author_id,\n    27→      listingId,\n    28→      \"new_member\",\n    29→      `${joinerName} joined your reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\"`\n    30→    );\n    31→  }\n    32→}\n    33→\n    34→export function notifyGroupFull(listingId: number) {\n    35→  const listing = db\n    36→    .prepare(\"SELECT book_title FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    37→    .get(listingId) as { book_title: string } | undefined;\n    38→  const members = db\n    39→    .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n    40→    .all(listingId) as { user_id: number }[];\n    41→\n    42→  if (listing) {\n    43→    for (const member of members) {\n    44→      createNotification(\n    45→        member.user_id,\n    46→        listingId,\n    47→        \"group_full\",\n    48→        `The reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" is now full! The organizer will share a Telegram link soon.`\n    49→      );\n    50→    }\n    51→  }\n    52→}\n    53→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"235c9f17-c672-4220-8162-6bda60e23e6c","uuid":"393c3da7-007d-4d77-ac50-708997a3ddfc","timestamp":"2026-03-19T23:03:06.233Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/notifications.ts","content":"import db from \"./db\";\n\nexport function createNotification(\n  userId: number,\n  listingId: number,\n  type: string,\n  message: string\n) {\n  db.prepare(\n    \"INSERT INTO notifications (user_id, listing_id, type, message) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)\"\n  ).run(userId, listingId, type, message);\n}\n\nexport function notifyListingAuthor(\n  listingId: number,\n  joinerName: string\n) {\n  const listing = db\n    .prepare(\n      \"SELECT l.author_id, l.book_title FROM listings l WHERE l.id = ?\"\n    )\n    .get(listingId) as { author_id: number; book_title: string } | undefined;\n\n  if (listing) {\n    createNotification(\n      listing.author_id,\n      listingId,\n      \"new_member\",\n      `${joinerName} joined your reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\"`\n    );\n  }\n}\n\nexport function notifyGroupFull(listingId: number) {\n  const listing = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT book_title FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    .get(listingId) as { book_title: string } | undefined;\n  const members = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n    .all(listingId) as { user_id: number }[];\n\n  if (listing) {\n    for (const member of members) {\n      createNotification(\n        member.user_id,\n        listingId,\n        \"group_full\",\n        `The reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" is now full! The organizer will share a Telegram link soon.`\n      );\n    }\n  }\n}\n","numLines":53,"startLine":1,"totalLines":53}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_014GfDz84vxL5VwMXG9xTNg6","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→\n     5→export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {\n     6→  const session = await requireAuth();\n     7→  if (!session) {\n     8→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n     9→  }\n    10→\n    11→  try {\n    12→    const body = await req.json();\n    13→    const {\n    14→      bookTitle,\n    15→      bookAuthor,\n    16→      bookCoverUrl,\n    17→      bookOlid,\n    18→      language,\n    19→      readingPace,\n    20→      startDate,\n    21→      meetingFormat,\n    22→      maxGroupSize,\n    23→    } = body;\n    24→\n    25→    if (!bookTitle || !bookAuthor || !readingPace || !startDate || !meetingFormat || !maxGroupSize) {\n    26→      return NextResponse.json(\n    27→        { error: \"Missing required fields\" },\n    28→        { status: 400 }\n    29→      );\n    30→    }\n    31→\n    32→    if (![\"voice\", \"text\", \"mixed\"].includes(meetingFormat)) {\n    33→      return NextResponse.json(\n    34→        { error: \"Invalid meeting format\" },\n    35→        { status: 400 }\n    36→      );\n    37→    }\n    38→\n    39→    const size = parseInt(maxGroupSize, 10);\n    40→    if (isNaN(size) || size < 2 || size > 20) {\n    41→      return NextResponse.json(\n    42→        { error: \"Group size must be between 2 and 20\" },\n    43→        { status: 400 }\n    44→      );\n    45→    }\n    46→\n    47→    const result = db\n    48→      .prepare(\n    49→        `INSERT INTO listings (author_id, book_title, book_author, book_cover_url, book_olid, language, reading_pace, start_date, meeting_format, max_group_size)\n    50→         VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`\n    51→      )\n    52→      .run(\n    53→        session.userId,\n    54→        bookTitle.trim(),\n    55→        bookAuthor.trim(),\n    56→        bookCoverUrl || \"\",\n    57→        bookOlid || \"\",\n    58→        language || \"English\",\n    59→        readingPace.trim(),\n    60→        startDate,\n    61→        meetingFormat,\n    62→        size\n    63→      );\n    64→\n    65→    // Auto-join the author as a member\n    66→    db.prepare(\n    67→      \"INSERT INTO listing_members (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\"\n    68→    ).run(result.lastInsertRowid, session.userId);\n    69→\n    70→    return NextResponse.json(\n    71→      { id: result.lastInsertRowid },\n    72→      { status: 201 }\n    73→    );\n    74→  } catch (error) {\n    75→    console.error(\"Create listing error:\", error);\n    76→    return NextResponse.json(\n    77→      { error: \"Internal server error\" },\n    78→      { status: 500 }\n    79→    );\n    80→  }\n    81→}\n    82→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"235c9f17-c672-4220-8162-6bda60e23e6c","uuid":"a2a62a48-7b1c-4410-83ce-7fbf8e745d03","timestamp":"2026-03-19T23:03:06.785Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/create/route.ts","content":"import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport db from \"@/lib/db\";\nimport { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n\nexport async function POST(req: NextRequest) {\n  const session = await requireAuth();\n  if (!session) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n  }\n\n  try {\n    const body = await req.json();\n    const {\n      bookTitle,\n      bookAuthor,\n      bookCoverUrl,\n      bookOlid,\n      language,\n      readingPace,\n      startDate,\n      meetingFormat,\n      maxGroupSize,\n    } = body;\n\n    if (!bookTitle || !bookAuthor || !readingPace || !startDate || !meetingFormat || !maxGroupSize) {\n      return NextResponse.json(\n        { error: \"Missing required fields\" },\n        { status: 400 }\n      );\n    }\n\n    if (![\"voice\", \"text\", \"mixed\"].includes(meetingFormat)) {\n      return NextResponse.json(\n        { error: \"Invalid meeting format\" },\n        { status: 400 }\n      );\n    }\n\n    const size = parseInt(maxGroupSize, 10);\n    if (isNaN(size) || size < 2 || size > 20) {\n      return NextResponse.json(\n        { error: \"Group size must be between 2 and 20\" },\n        { status: 400 }\n      );\n    }\n\n    const result = db\n      .prepare(\n        `INSERT INTO listings (author_id, book_title, book_author, book_cover_url, book_olid, language, reading_pace, start_date, meeting_format, max_group_size)\n         VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`\n      )\n      .run(\n        session.userId,\n        bookTitle.trim(),\n        bookAuthor.trim(),\n        bookCoverUrl || \"\",\n        bookOlid || \"\",\n        language || \"English\",\n        readingPace.trim(),\n        startDate,\n        meetingFormat,\n        size\n      );\n\n    // Auto-join the author as a member\n    db.prepare(\n      \"INSERT INTO listing_members (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\"\n    ).run(result.lastInsertRowid, session.userId);\n\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { id: result.lastInsertRowid },\n      { status: 201 }\n    );\n  } catch (error) {\n    console.error(\"Create listing error:\", error);\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Internal server error\" },\n      { status: 500 }\n    );\n  }\n}\n","numLines":82,"startLine":1,"totalLines":82}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01TmaahpkeBrNVFbvzphRCK9","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { notifyListingAuthor, notifyGroupFull } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n     5→\n     6→export async function POST(\n     7→  _req: NextRequest,\n     8→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n     9→) {\n    10→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    11→  if (!session) {\n    12→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    13→  }\n    14→\n    15→  const { id } = await params;\n    16→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    17→\n    18→  const listing = db\n    19→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    20→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    21→\n    22→  if (!listing) {\n    23→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    24→  }\n    25→\n    26→  if (listing.is_full) {\n    27→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"This group is already full\" }, { status: 400 });\n    28→  }\n    29→\n    30→  // Check if already a member\n    31→  const existing = db\n    32→    .prepare(\n    33→      \"SELECT 1 FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ?\"\n    34→    )\n    35→    .get(listingId, session.userId);\n    36→\n    37→  if (existing) {\n    38→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"You are already a member\" }, { status: 400 });\n    39→  }\n    40→\n    41→  // Count current members\n    42→  const { count } = db\n    43→    .prepare(\"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n    44→    .get(listingId) as { count: number };\n    45→\n    46→  if (count >= (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n    47→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"This group is already full\" }, { status: 400 });\n    48→  }\n    49→\n    50→  // Join\n    51→  db.prepare(\n    52→    \"INSERT INTO listing_members (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\"\n    53→  ).run(listingId, session.userId);\n    54→\n    55→  // Notify the author\n    56→  notifyListingAuthor(listingId, session.displayName || \"Someone\");\n    57→\n    58→  // Check if group is now full\n    59→  const newCount = count + 1;\n    60→  if (newCount >= (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n    61→    db.prepare(\"UPDATE listings SET is_full = 1 WHERE id = ?\").run(listingId);\n    62→    notifyGroupFull(listingId);\n    63→  }\n    64→\n    65→  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true, memberCount: newCount });\n    66→}\n    67→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"235c9f17-c672-4220-8162-6bda60e23e6c","uuid":"3128f037-a112-4217-af60-7e241b69d7a2","timestamp":"2026-03-19T23:03:07.044Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts","content":"import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport db from \"@/lib/db\";\nimport { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\nimport { notifyListingAuthor, notifyGroupFull } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n\nexport async function POST(\n  _req: NextRequest,\n  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n) {\n  const session = await requireAuth();\n  if (!session) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n  }\n\n  const { id } = await params;\n  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n\n  const listing = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n\n  if (!listing) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n  }\n\n  if (listing.is_full) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"This group is already full\" }, { status: 400 });\n  }\n\n  // Check if already a member\n  const existing = db\n    .prepare(\n      \"SELECT 1 FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ?\"\n    )\n    .get(listingId, session.userId);\n\n  if (existing) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"You are already a member\" }, { status: 400 });\n  }\n\n  // Count current members\n  const { count } = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n    .get(listingId) as { count: number };\n\n  if (count >= (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"This group is already full\" }, { status: 400 });\n  }\n\n  // Join\n  db.prepare(\n    \"INSERT INTO listing_members (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\"\n  ).run(listingId, session.userId);\n\n  // Notify the author\n  notifyListingAuthor(listingId, session.displayName || \"Someone\");\n\n  // Check if group is now full\n  const newCount = count + 1;\n  if (newCount >= (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n    db.prepare(\"UPDATE listings SET is_full = 1 WHERE id = ?\").run(listingId);\n    notifyGroupFull(listingId);\n  }\n\n  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true, memberCount: newCount });\n}\n","numLines":67,"startLine":1,"totalLines":67}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01MVY2LXzfGfFXTbiEh73bPn","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { getSession } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→\n     5→export async function GET(\n     6→  _req: NextRequest,\n     7→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n     8→) {\n     9→  const { id } = await params;\n    10→\n    11→  const listing = db\n    12→    .prepare(\n    13→      `SELECT\n    14→        l.*,\n    15→        u.display_name as author_name\n    16→      FROM listings l\n    17→      JOIN users u ON l.author_id = u.id\n    18→      WHERE l.id = ?`\n    19→    )\n    20→    .get(id) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    21→\n    22→  if (!listing) {\n    23→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    24→  }\n    25→\n    26→  const members = db\n    27→    .prepare(\n    28→      `SELECT u.id, u.display_name, u.bio, lm.joined_at\n    29→       FROM listing_members lm\n    30→       JOIN users u ON lm.user_id = u.id\n    31→       WHERE lm.listing_id = ?\n    32→       ORDER BY lm.joined_at ASC`\n    33→    )\n    34→    .all(id) as { id: number; display_name: string; bio: string; joined_at: string }[];\n    35→\n    36→  const session = await getSession();\n    37→  const isMember = session.userId\n    38→    ? members.some((m) => m.id === session.userId)\n    39→    : false;\n    40→  const isAuthor = session.userId\n    41→    ? listing.author_id === session.userId\n    42→    : false;\n    43→\n    44→  return NextResponse.json({\n    45→    listing: {\n    46→      ...listing,\n    47→      members,\n    48→      memberCount: members.length,\n    49→      isMember,\n    50→      isAuthor,\n    51→    },\n    52→  });\n    53→}\n    54→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"235c9f17-c672-4220-8162-6bda60e23e6c","uuid":"5d237f9e-fd2d-4915-9b91-bdd7bc39e672","timestamp":"2026-03-19T23:03:07.324Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts","content":"import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport db from \"@/lib/db\";\nimport { getSession } from \"@/lib/session\";\n\nexport async function GET(\n  _req: NextRequest,\n  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n) {\n  const { id } = await params;\n\n  const listing = db\n    .prepare(\n      `SELECT\n        l.*,\n        u.display_name as author_name\n      FROM listings l\n      JOIN users u ON l.author_id = u.id\n      WHERE l.id = ?`\n    )\n    .get(id) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n\n  if (!listing) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n  }\n\n  const members = db\n    .prepare(\n      `SELECT u.id, u.display_name, u.bio, lm.joined_at\n       FROM listing_members lm\n       JOIN users u ON lm.user_id = u.id\n       WHERE lm.listing_id = ?\n       ORDER BY lm.joined_at ASC`\n    )\n    .all(id) as { id: number; display_name: string; bio: string; joined_at: string }[];\n\n  const session = await getSession();\n  const isMember = session.userId\n    ? members.some((m) => m.id === session.userId)\n    : false;\n  const isAuthor = session.userId\n    ? listing.author_id === session.userId\n    : false;\n\n  return NextResponse.json({\n    listing: {\n      ...listing,\n      members,\n      memberCount: members.length,\n      isMember,\n      isAuthor,\n    },\n  });\n}\n","numLines":54,"startLine":1,"totalLines":54}}}

client\";\n\nimport { useState } from \"react\";\nimport { useRouter } from \"next/navigation\";\nimport BookSearch from \"@/components/BookSearch\";\n\nexport default function CreateListingPage() {\n  const router = useRouter();\n  const [error, setError] = useState(\"\");\n  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false);\n\n  const [bookTitle, setBookTitle] = useState(\"\");\n  const [bookAuthor, setBookAuthor] = useState(\"\");\n  const [bookCoverUrl, setBookCoverUrl] = useState(\"\");\n  const [bookOlid, setBookOlid] = useState(\"\");\n  const [language, setLanguage] = useState(\"English\");\n  const [readingPace, setReadingPace] = useState(\"\");\n  const [startDate, setStartDate] = useState(\"\");\n  const [meetingFormat, setMeetingFormat] = useState(\"text\");\n  const [maxGroupSize, setMaxGroupSize] = useState(\"4\");\n\n  const handleBookSelect = (book: {\n    title: string;\n    author: string;\n    coverUrl: string;\n    olid: string;\n  }) => {\n    setBookTitle(book.title);\n    setBookAuthor(book.author);\n    setBookCoverUrl(book.coverUrl);\n    setBookOlid(book.olid);\n  };\n\n  const handleSubmit = async (e: React.FormEvent) => {\n    e.preventDefault();\n    setError(\"\");\n    setLoading(true);\n\n    try {\n      const res = await fetch(\"/api/listings/create\", {\n        method: \"POST\",\n        headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n        body: JSON.stringify({\n          bookTitle,\n          bookAuthor,\n          bookCoverUrl,\n          bookOlid,\n          language,\n          readingPace,\n          startDate,\n          meetingFormat,\n          maxGroupSize,\n        }),\n      });\n\n      const data = await res.json();\n\n      if (!res.ok) {\n        if (res.status === 401) {\n          router.push(\"/auth/login\");\n          return;\n        }\n        setError(data.error || \"Failed to create listing\");\n        return;\n      }\n\n      router.push(`/listings/${data.id}`);\n    } catch {\n      setError(\"Something went wrong. Please try again.\");\n    } finally {\n      setLoading(false);\n    }\n  };\n\n  return (\n    <div className=\"max-w-lg mx-auto\">\n      <h1 className=\"text-3xl mb-2\">Start a reading group</h1>\n      <p\n        className=\"mb-8\"\n        style={{\n          color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n          fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\",\n          fontSize: \"0.95rem\",\n        }}\n      >\n        Choose a book, set your pace, and wait for readers to join.\n      </p>\n\n      {error && (\n        <div\n          className=\"mb-4 p-3 rounded-lg text-sm\"\n          style={{\n            backgroundColor: \"rgba(192, 57, 43, 0.08)\",\n            color: \"var(--color-error)\",\n            fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\",\n          }}\n        >\n          {error}\n        </div>\n      )}\n\n      <form onSubmit={handleSubmit} className=\"space-y-5\">\n        <div>\n          <label\n            className=\"block text-sm font-semibold mb-1\"\n            style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n          >\n            Book\n          </label>\n          <BookSearch onSelect={handleBookSelect} />\n          {bookTitle && (\n            <div\n              className=\"mt-2 flex items-center gap-3 p-3 rounded-lg\"\n              style={{ backgroundColor: \"rgba(224, 122, 58, 0.06)\" }}\n            >\n              {bookCoverUrl && (\n                <img\n                  src={bookCoverUrl}\n                  alt=\"\"\n                  className=\"w-10 h-14 object-cover rounded\"\n                />\n              )}\n              <div>\n                <div\n                  className=\"text-sm font-semibold\"\n                  style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n                >\n                  {bookTitle}\n                </div>\n                <div\n                  className=\"text-xs\"\n                  style={{\n                    color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n                    fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\",\n                  }}\n                >\n                  by {bookAuthor}\n                </div>\n              </div>\n            </div>\n          )}\n        </div>\n\n        <div>\n          <label\n            className=\"block text-sm font-semibold mb-1\"\n            style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n          >\n            Discussion language\n          </label>\n          <select\n            className=\"input-field\"\n            value={language}\n            onChange={(e) => setLanguage(e.target.value)}\n          >\n            <option>English</option>\n            <option>Spanish</option>\n            <option>French</option>\n            <option>German</option>\n            <option>Russian</option>\n            <option>Portuguese</option>\n            <option>Italian</option>\n            <option>Japanese</option>\n            <option>Chinese</option>\n            <option>Korean</option>\n            <option>Other</option>\n          </select>\n        </div>\n\n        <div>\n          <label\n            className=\"block text-sm font-semibold mb-1\"\n            style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n          >\n            Reading pace\n          </label>\n          <input\n            type=\"text\"\n            className=\"input-field\"\n            value={readingPace}\n            onChange={(e) => setReadingPace(e.target.value)}\n            required\n            placeholder='e.g. \"1 chapter per week\" or \"50 pages per day\"'\n          />\n        </div>\n\n        <div>\n          <label\n            className=\"block text-sm font-semibold mb-1\"\n            style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n          >\n            Start date\n          </label>\n          <input\n            type=\"date\"\n            className=\"input-field\"\n            value={startDate}\n            onChange={(e) => setStartDate(e.target.value)}\n            required\n          />\n        </div>\n\n        <div>\n          <label\n            className=\"block text-sm font-semibold mb-1\"\n            style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n          >\n            Meeting format\n          </label>\n          <div className=\"flex gap-3\">\n            {[\n              { value: \"text\", label: \"Text chat\" },\n              { value: \"voice\", label: \"Voice calls\" },\n              { value: \"mixed\", label: \"Mixed\" },\n            ].map((opt) => (\n              <button\n                key={opt.value}\n                type=\"button\"\n                onClick={() => setMeetingFormat(opt.value)}\n                className=\"flex-1 py-2 px-3 rounded-lg text-sm font-semibold transition-colors cursor-pointer\"\n                style={{\n                  fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\",\n                  backgroundColor:\n                    meetingFormat === opt.value\n                      ? \"var(--color-accent)\"\n                      : \"transparent\",\n                  color:\n                    meetingFormat === opt.value\n                      ? \"white\"\n                      : \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n                  border:\n                    meetingFormat === opt.value\n                      ? \"1.5px solid var(--color-accent)\"\n                      : \"1.5px solid var(--color-border)\",\n                }}\n              >\n                {opt.label}\n              </button>\n            ))}\n          </div>\n        </div>\n\n        <div>\n          <label\n            className=\"block text-sm font-semibold mb-1\"\n            style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n          >\n            Maximum group size\n          </label>\n          <select\n            className=\"input-field\"\n            value={maxGroupSize}\n            onChange={(e) => setMaxGroupSize(e.target.value)}\n          >\n            {[2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 15, 20].map((n) => (\n              <option key={n} value={n}>\n                {n} readers\n              </option>\n            ))}\n          </select>\n        </div>\n\n        <button\n          type=\"submit\"\n          className=\"btn-primary w-full\"\n          disabled={loading || !bookTitle}\n        >\n          {loading ? \"Creating...\" : \"Create reading group\"}\n        </button>\n      </form>\n    </div>\n  );\n}\n","numLines":274,"startLine":1,"totalLines":274}}}
